scope: This specification covers the reviewed RFC 9000 transport extraction outputs across all temporary batch ranges while collapsing duplicate batch coverage into one canonical RFC-level specification.
purpose: Capture the transport requirements defined by RFC 9000 as canonical spec-trace clauses.
context: RFC 9000 is the largest QUIC source in this assembly. The temporary extraction batches overlap in places, especially around sections 6 through 10, so the assembly preserves section-level provenance while deduplicating only exact same-section duplicates.

requirements.title: Give streams an ordered byte-stream abstraction
requirements.statement: Streams in QUIC MUST provide applications with an ordered byte-stream abstraction.
notes: The word "lightweight" is descriptive and not separately elevated.

requirements.title: Permit streams to be created by sending data
requirements.statement: Streams in QUIC MAY be created by sending data.

requirements.title: Allow streams to be long-lived
requirements.statement: Streams in QUIC MAY last for the entire duration of a connection.

requirements.title: Allow either endpoint to create streams
requirements.statement: Streams in QUIC MAY be created by either endpoint.

requirements.title: Allow interleaved concurrent stream data
requirements.statement: Streams in QUIC MAY concurrently send data interleaved with other streams.

requirements.title: Allow streams to be canceled
requirements.statement: Streams in QUIC MAY be canceled.

requirements.title: Avoid cross-stream byte ordering guarantees
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST NOT provide ordering between bytes on different streams.

requirements.title: Allow arbitrary concurrent streams
requirements.statement: QUIC MAY allow an arbitrary number of streams to operate concurrently.

requirements.title: Allow arbitrary per-stream data subject to limits
requirements.statement: QUIC MAY allow an arbitrary amount of data to be sent on any stream, subject to flow control constraints and stream limits.

requirements.title: Constrain unidirectional stream direction
requirements.statement: A unidirectional stream MUST carry data only from the initiator of the stream to its peer.

requirements.title: Constrain bidirectional stream direction
requirements.statement: A bidirectional stream MUST allow data to be sent in both directions.

requirements.title: Identify streams by stream ID
requirements.statement: Each stream MUST be identified within a connection by a numeric value called the stream ID.

requirements.title: Bound stream IDs to 62 bits
requirements.statement: A stream ID MUST be a 62-bit integer in the range 0 to 2^62-1.
notes: The ledger renders the numeric expression without the exponent marker; the RFC text intends the 62-bit range.

requirements.title: Keep stream IDs unique per connection
requirements.statement: A stream ID MUST be unique for all streams on a connection.

requirements.title: Encode stream IDs as variable-length integers
requirements.statement: Stream IDs MUST be encoded as variable-length integers.

requirements.title: Forbid stream ID reuse
requirements.statement: A QUIC endpoint MUST NOT reuse a stream ID within a connection.

requirements.title: Use the low bit to identify stream initiator
requirements.statement: The least significant bit of a stream ID MUST identify the initiator of the stream.

requirements.title: Use even IDs for client-initiated streams
requirements.statement: Client-initiated streams MUST have even-numbered stream IDs with the least significant bit set to 0.

requirements.title: Use odd IDs for server-initiated streams
requirements.statement: Server-initiated streams MUST have odd-numbered stream IDs with the least significant bit set to 1.

requirements.title: Use the second low bit to distinguish stream direction
requirements.statement: The second least significant bit of a stream ID MUST distinguish bidirectional streams from unidirectional streams, with 0 indicating bidirectional and 1 indicating unidirectional.

requirements.title: Start each stream space at its minimum value
requirements.statement: The stream space for each type MUST begin at its minimum value.
notes: The source gives the minima as 0x00 through 0x03, respectively.

requirements.title: Increase stream IDs numerically
requirements.statement: Successive streams of each type MUST be created with numerically increasing stream IDs.

requirements.title: Open lower-numbered streams when a stream ID is used out of order
requirements.statement: Using a stream ID out of order MUST cause all lower-numbered streams of that type to be opened.

requirements.title: Encapsulate application data in STREAM frames
requirements.statement: STREAM frames MUST encapsulate data sent by an application.

requirements.title: Use stream and offset fields to order data
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST use the Stream ID and Offset fields in STREAM frames to place data in order.

requirements.title: Deliver stream data as an ordered byte stream
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST be able to deliver stream data to an application as an ordered byte stream.

requirements.title: Buffer out-of-order stream data until it can be delivered in order
requirements.statement: To deliver an ordered byte stream, an endpoint MUST buffer any out-of-order data it receives up to the advertised flow control limit.

requirements.title: Allow optional out-of-order delivery to applications
requirements.statement: Implementations MAY offer the ability to deliver stream data out of order to a receiving application.

requirements.title: Permit discarded received data
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY discard data that has already been received.

requirements.title: Keep retransmitted stream data stable at the same offset
requirements.statement: The data at a given offset MUST NOT change if it is sent multiple times.

requirements.title: Permit treating conflicting retransmissions as protocol violations
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY treat receipt of different data at the same offset within a stream as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

requirements.title: Expose streams as ordered byte streams only
requirements.statement: Streams MUST be an ordered byte-stream abstraction with no other structure visible to QUIC.

requirements.title: Respect peer flow control limits before sending
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT send data on any stream without ensuring that it is within the flow control limits set by its peer.

requirements.title: Avoid a built-in prioritization exchange
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST NOT provide a mechanism for exchanging prioritization information.

requirements.title: Provide application-visible stream priority controls
requirements.statement: A QUIC implementation SHOULD provide ways in which an application can indicate the relative priority of streams.

requirements.title: Use application priority information for resource allocation
requirements.statement: A QUIC implementation SHOULD use information provided by the application to determine how to allocate resources to active streams.
notes: The source splits the normative statement and the explanatory follow-on into one ledger entry.

requirements.title: Provide the stream operations described here
requirements.statement: A QUIC implementation MUST provide an interface that includes the operations described in this section.

requirements.title: Allow protocol-specific implementations to expose only needed operations
requirements.statement: An implementation designed for use with a specific application protocol MAY provide only those operations that are used by that protocol.

requirements.title: Allow writing data after reserving flow control credit
requirements.statement: An application protocol MAY write data only after stream flow control credit has been successfully reserved to send the written data.

requirements.title: Allow clean stream termination
requirements.statement: An application protocol MAY end a stream, resulting in a STREAM frame with the FIN bit set.

requirements.title: Allow abrupt stream termination
requirements.statement: An application protocol MAY reset a stream if the stream is not already in a terminal state, resulting in a RESET_STREAM frame.

requirements.title: Allow reading data
requirements.statement: An application protocol MAY read data from a stream.

requirements.title: Allow aborting stream reads
requirements.statement: An application protocol MAY abort reading a stream and request closure, possibly resulting in a STOP_SENDING frame.

requirements.title: Allow stream state-change notifications
requirements.statement: An application protocol MAY request to be informed of stream state changes, including when the peer has opened or reset a stream, when a peer aborts reading on a stream, when new data is available, and when data can or cannot be written to the stream due to flow control.

requirements.title: Choose the correct state machine for unidirectional streams
requirements.statement: Unidirectional streams MUST use either the sending or receiving state machine, depending on the stream type and endpoint role.

requirements.title: Use both state machines for bidirectional streams
requirements.statement: Bidirectional streams MUST use both state machines at both endpoints.

requirements.title: Allow alternate stream state machines with consistent behavior
requirements.statement: An implementation MAY define a different stream state machine as long as its behavior is consistent with an implementation that implements these states.

requirements.title: Treat Ready as a newly created sendable stream
requirements.statement: The Ready state MUST represent a newly created stream that is able to accept data from the application.

requirements.title: Permit buffering in Ready
requirements.statement: An implementation MAY buffer stream data in the Ready state in preparation for sending.

requirements.title: Enter Send state on first outbound stream frame
requirements.statement: Sending the first STREAM or STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame MUST cause a sending part of a stream to enter the Send state.

requirements.title: Allow deferred stream ID allocation
requirements.statement: An implementation MAY defer allocating a stream ID to a stream until it sends the first STREAM frame and enters the Send state.

requirements.title: Start peer-initiated bidirectional senders in Ready
requirements.statement: The sending part of a bidirectional stream initiated by a peer MUST start in the Ready state when the receiving part is created.

requirements.title: Transmit and retransmit in Send
requirements.statement: In the Send state, an endpoint MUST transmit and retransmit as necessary stream data in STREAM frames.

requirements.title: Respect peer flow control while sending
requirements.statement: An endpoint in the Send state MUST respect the flow control limits set by its peer and continue to accept and process MAX_STREAM_DATA frames.

requirements.title: Signal Send blocking with STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED
requirements.statement: An endpoint in the Send state MUST generate STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frames if it is blocked from sending by stream flow control limits.

requirements.title: Enter Data Sent after sending FIN
requirements.statement: After the application indicates that all stream data has been sent and a STREAM frame containing the FIN bit is sent, the sending part of the stream MUST enter the Data Sent state.

requirements.title: Restrict Data Sent retransmissions
requirements.statement: From the Data Sent state, the endpoint MUST retransmit stream data only as necessary.

requirements.title: Stop flow control checks in Data Sent
requirements.statement: An endpoint in the Data Sent state MUST NOT need to check flow control limits or send STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frames for the stream.

requirements.title: Allow ignoring MAX_STREAM_DATA in Data Sent
requirements.statement: An endpoint in the Data Sent state MAY safely ignore any MAX_STREAM_DATA frames it receives from its peer for that stream.

requirements.title: Enter Data Recvd after all data is acknowledged
requirements.statement: Once all stream data has been successfully acknowledged, the sending part of the stream MUST enter the Data Recvd state.

requirements.title: Allow application abandonment of stream transmission
requirements.statement: From any state that is Ready, Send, or Data Sent, an application MAY signal that it wishes to abandon transmission of stream data.

requirements.title: Send RESET_STREAM when abandoning transmission
requirements.statement: In either case, the endpoint MUST send a RESET_STREAM frame, which causes the stream to enter the Reset Sent state.

requirements.title: Permit RESET_STREAM as the first frame
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY send a RESET_STREAM as the first frame that mentions a stream, causing the sending part of that stream to open and then immediately transition to the Reset Sent state.

requirements.title: Enter Reset Recvd after RESET_STREAM is acknowledged
requirements.statement: Once a packet containing a RESET_STREAM has been acknowledged, the sending part of the stream MUST enter the Reset Recvd state.

requirements.title: Do not track unobservable sending-part states
requirements.statement: The receiving part of a stream MUST NOT track states on the sending part that cannot be observed, such as the Ready state.

requirements.title: Track data delivery to the application
requirements.statement: The receiving part of a stream MUST track the delivery of data to the application, including data that cannot be observed by the sender.

requirements.title: Create peer-initiated receiving parts on first frame
requirements.statement: The receiving part of a stream initiated by a peer MUST be created when the first STREAM, STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED, or RESET_STREAM frame is received for that stream.

requirements.title: Create bidirectional receiving parts on peer flow-control or stop-sending frames
requirements.statement: For bidirectional streams initiated by a peer, receipt of a MAX_STREAM_DATA or STOP_SENDING frame for the sending part of the stream MUST also create the receiving part.

requirements.title: Start receiving parts in Recv
requirements.statement: The initial state for the receiving part of a stream MUST be Recv.

requirements.title: Enter Recv when the peer-side sending part opens
requirements.statement: For a bidirectional stream, the receiving part MUST enter the Recv state when the sending part initiated by the endpoint enters the Ready state.

requirements.title: Open bidirectional streams on peer flow-control or stop-sending frames
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST open a bidirectional stream when a MAX_STREAM_DATA or STOP_SENDING frame is received from the peer for that stream.

requirements.title: Interpret MAX_STREAM_DATA on an unopened stream as peer opening
requirements.statement: Receiving a MAX_STREAM_DATA frame for an unopened stream MUST indicate that the remote peer has opened the stream and is providing flow control credit.

requirements.title: Interpret STOP_SENDING on an unopened stream as a peer desire to stop reception
requirements.statement: Receiving a STOP_SENDING frame for an unopened stream MUST indicate that the remote peer no longer wishes to receive data on this stream.

requirements.title: Allow reordered peer flow-control or stop-sending frames
requirements.statement: Either frame MAY arrive before a STREAM or STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame if packets are lost or reordered.

requirements.title: Open lower-numbered peer streams first
requirements.statement: Before a stream is created, all streams of the same type with lower-numbered stream IDs MUST be created.

requirements.title: Receive STREAM and STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED in Recv
requirements.statement: In the Recv state, the endpoint MUST receive STREAM and STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frames.

requirements.title: Buffer received stream data for ordered delivery
requirements.statement: Incoming data MUST be buffered for ordered delivery to the application, including any reassembly needed to place it in the correct order.

requirements.title: Advertise more receive credit as data is consumed
requirements.statement: As data is consumed by the application and buffer space becomes available, the endpoint MUST send MAX_STREAM_DATA frames to allow the peer to send more data.

requirements.title: Recognize final size when FIN arrives
requirements.statement: When a STREAM frame with a FIN bit is received, the final size of the stream MUST be known.

requirements.title: Enter Size Known after FIN
requirements.statement: The receiving part of the stream MUST then enter the Size Known state.

requirements.title: Enter Data Recvd after all data arrives
requirements.statement: Once all data for the stream has been received, the receiving part MUST enter the Data Recvd state.

requirements.title: Discard further stream frames after all data is received
requirements.statement: After all data has been received, any STREAM or STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frames for the stream MAY be discarded.

requirements.title: Keep Data Recvd until the application receives the data
requirements.statement: The Data Recvd state MUST persist until stream data has been delivered to the application.

requirements.title: Enter Data Read after delivery to the application
requirements.statement: Once stream data has been delivered, the stream MUST enter the Data Read state.

requirements.title: Enter Reset Recvd on RESET_STREAM in Recv or Size Known
requirements.statement: Receiving a RESET_STREAM frame in the Recv or Size Known state MUST cause the stream to enter the Reset Recvd state.

requirements.title: Allow interruption of delivery and signal RESET_STREAM
requirements.statement: An implementation MAY interrupt delivery of stream data, discard any data that was not consumed, and signal the receipt of the RESET_STREAM.

requirements.title: Remain in Data Recvd when RESET_STREAM is suppressed
requirements.statement: If the RESET_STREAM is suppressed, the receiving part of the stream MUST remain in Data Recvd.

requirements.title: Enter Reset Read after the application receives reset notification
requirements.statement: Once the application receives the signal indicating that the stream was reset, the receiving part of the stream MUST transition to the Reset Read state.

requirements.title: Forbid terminal-state transmit frames
requirements.statement: A sender MUST NOT send STREAM, STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED, or RESET_STREAM frames from a terminal state.

requirements.title: Forbid post-reset transmission of STREAM frames
requirements.statement: A sender MUST NOT send a STREAM or STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame for a stream in the Reset Sent state or any terminal state after sending a RESET_STREAM frame.

requirements.title: Allow delayed receipt of state-changing frames
requirements.statement: A receiver MAY receive STREAM, STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED, or RESET_STREAM frames in any state due to delayed delivery of packets carrying them.

requirements.title: Limit MAX_STREAM_DATA to Recv
requirements.statement: The receiver MUST only send MAX_STREAM_DATA frames in the Recv state.

requirements.title: Allow STOP_SENDING except after reset reception
requirements.statement: A receiver MAY send a STOP_SENDING frame in any state where it has not received a RESET_STREAM frame.

requirements.title: Allow delayed receipt of receiver-side frames
requirements.statement: A sender MAY receive MAX_STREAM_DATA or STOP_SENDING frames in any state as a result of delayed delivery of packets.

requirements.title: Compose bidirectional streams from send and receive parts
requirements.statement: A bidirectional stream MUST be composed of sending and receiving parts.

requirements.title: Allow composite stream-state representations
requirements.statement: Implementations MAY represent bidirectional stream states as composites of sending and receiving stream states.

requirements.title: Require acknowledgment before closed-state transitions in the example mapping
requirements.statement: Any mapping to a closed or half-closed state MUST require data to be acknowledged before the transition.

requirements.title: Allow aborting stream reads with an application error code
requirements.statement: If an application is no longer interested in the data it is receiving on a stream, it MAY abort reading the stream and specify an application error code.

requirements.title: Signal aborting reads with STOP_SENDING
requirements.statement: If the stream is in the Recv or Size Known state, the transport SHOULD signal aborting reading by sending a STOP_SENDING frame to prompt closure of the stream in the opposite direction.

requirements.title: Count data after STOP_SENDING toward flow control
requirements.statement: STREAM frames received after sending a STOP_SENDING frame MUST still be counted toward connection and stream flow control even if they are discarded upon receipt.

requirements.title: Treat STOP_SENDING as a request for RESET_STREAM
requirements.statement: A STOP_SENDING frame MUST request that the receiving endpoint send a RESET_STREAM frame.

requirements.title: Send RESET_STREAM after receiving STOP_SENDING in Ready or Send
requirements.statement: An endpoint that receives a STOP_SENDING frame MUST send a RESET_STREAM frame if the stream is in the Ready or Send state.

requirements.title: Permit deferred RESET_STREAM in Data Sent
requirements.statement: If the stream is in the Data Sent state, the endpoint MAY defer sending the RESET_STREAM frame until the packets containing outstanding data are acknowledged or declared lost.

requirements.title: Prefer RESET_STREAM over retransmission after loss
requirements.statement: If any outstanding data is declared lost, the endpoint SHOULD send a RESET_STREAM frame instead of retransmitting the data.

requirements.title: Copy STOP_SENDING error codes when sending RESET_STREAM
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD copy the error code from the STOP_SENDING frame to the RESET_STREAM frame it sends.
notes: The source also permits using any application error code.

requirements.title: Allow ignoring later RESET_STREAM error codes
requirements.statement: An endpoint that sends a STOP_SENDING frame MAY ignore the error code in any RESET_STREAM frames subsequently received for that stream.

requirements.title: Prefer STOP_SENDING only for streams that have not been reset
requirements.statement: STOP_SENDING SHOULD only be sent for a stream that has not been reset by the peer.

requirements.title: Repeat STOP_SENDING after loss
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD send another STOP_SENDING frame if a packet containing a previous STOP_SENDING is lost.

requirements.title: Allow RESET_STREAM to terminate one direction of a bidirectional stream
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY terminate one direction of a bidirectional stream by sending a RESET_STREAM frame.

requirements.title: Allow STOP_SENDING to encourage prompt opposite-direction termination
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY encourage prompt termination in the opposite direction by sending a STOP_SENDING frame.

requirements.title: Flow-control streams and the whole connection
requirements.statement: Streams MUST be flow controlled both individually and across a connection as a whole.

requirements.title: Limit bytes on streams and across the connection
requirements.statement: A QUIC receiver MUST control the maximum amount of data the sender can send on a stream and across all streams at any time.

requirements.title: Control how many streams a peer can initiate
requirements.statement: A QUIC endpoint MUST control the maximum cumulative number of streams that its peer can initiate.

requirements.title: Exclude CRYPTO frames from stream-style flow control
requirements.statement: Data sent in CRYPTO frames MUST NOT be flow controlled in the same way as stream data.

requirements.title: Expose cryptographic buffering limits
requirements.statement: QUIC implementations SHOULD provide an interface for the cryptographic protocol implementation to communicate its buffering limits.

requirements.title: Use limit-based flow control
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST employ a limit-based flow control scheme in which a receiver advertises the total bytes it is prepared to receive on a given stream or for the entire connection.

requirements.title: Limit per-stream receive buffer use
requirements.statement: Stream flow control MUST prevent a single stream from consuming the entire receive buffer for a connection by limiting the amount of data that can be sent on each stream.

requirements.title: Limit total stream-data bytes on the connection
requirements.statement: Connection flow control MUST prevent senders from exceeding a receiver's buffer capacity for the connection by limiting the total bytes of stream data sent in STREAM frames on all streams.

requirements.title: Never send beyond either flow-control limit
requirements.statement: Senders MUST NOT send data in excess of either limit.

requirements.title: Set initial flow-control limits in transport parameters
requirements.statement: A receiver MUST set initial limits for all streams through transport parameters during the handshake.

requirements.title: Advertise larger flow-control limits with MAX frames
requirements.statement: Subsequently, a receiver MUST send MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frames to advertise larger limits.

requirements.title: Advertise larger stream limits with MAX_STREAM_DATA
requirements.statement: A receiver MAY advertise a larger limit for a stream by sending a MAX_STREAM_DATA frame with the corresponding stream ID.

requirements.title: Make MAX_STREAM_DATA indicate an absolute stream offset
requirements.statement: A MAX_STREAM_DATA frame MUST indicate the maximum absolute byte offset of a stream.

requirements.title: Advertise larger connection limits with MAX_DATA
requirements.statement: A receiver MAY advertise a larger limit for a connection by sending a MAX_DATA frame.

requirements.title: Track cumulative bytes for connection flow control
requirements.statement: A receiver MUST maintain a cumulative sum of bytes received on all streams to check for violations of the advertised connection or stream data limits.

requirements.title: Ignore smaller advertised limits
requirements.statement: Once a receiver advertises a limit for the connection or a stream, advertising a smaller limit MUST have no effect rather than be treated as an error.

requirements.title: Close on flow-control violations
requirements.statement: A receiver MUST close the connection with an error of type FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR if the sender violates the advertised connection or stream data limits.

requirements.title: Ignore non-increasing MAX frames
requirements.statement: A sender MUST ignore any MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frames that do not increase flow control limits.

requirements.title: Signal blocked senders
requirements.statement: A sender SHOULD send a STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED or DATA_BLOCKED frame to indicate to the receiver that it has data to write but is blocked by flow control limits.

requirements.title: Periodically signal blocking while flow-control limited
requirements.statement: A sender that is flow control limited SHOULD periodically send a STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED or DATA_BLOCKED frame when it has no ack-eliciting packets in flight.

requirements.title: Allow repeated or early credit advertisements
requirements.statement: A receiver MAY send a MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frame multiple times within a round trip or send it early enough to allow time for loss of the frame and subsequent recovery.

requirements.title: Allow autotuning of flow-control credit
requirements.statement: A receiver MAY use an autotuning mechanism to tune the frequency and amount of advertised additional credit based on a round-trip time estimate and the rate at which the receiving application consumes data.

requirements.title: Send flow-control frames opportunistically
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY send frames related to flow control only when there are other frames to send, ensuring that flow control does not cause extra packets to be sent.

requirements.title: Do not require blocked senders to signal blocking
requirements.statement: A blocked sender MUST NOT be required to send STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED or DATA_BLOCKED frames.

requirements.title: Do not wait for blocked signals before sending credit
requirements.statement: A receiver MUST NOT wait for a STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED or DATA_BLOCKED frame before sending a MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frame.

requirements.title: Tear down state and ignore later data after RESET_STREAM
requirements.statement: On receipt of a RESET_STREAM frame, an endpoint MUST tear down state for the matching stream and ignore further data arriving on that stream.

requirements.title: Terminate only one direction on RESET_STREAM
requirements.statement: RESET_STREAM MUST terminate one direction of a stream abruptly.

requirements.title: Leave the opposite direction unaffected
requirements.statement: For a bidirectional stream, RESET_STREAM MUST have no effect on data flow in the opposite direction.

requirements.title: Keep flow-control state for the unterminated direction
requirements.statement: Both endpoints MUST maintain flow control state for the stream in the unterminated direction until that direction enters a terminal state.

requirements.title: Communicate final size reliably
requirements.statement: A sender MUST always communicate the final size of a stream to the receiver reliably, no matter how the stream is terminated.

requirements.title: Use STREAM FIN to carry final size
requirements.statement: The final size of a stream MUST be the sum of the Offset and Length fields of a STREAM frame with a FIN flag, including any implicit values.

requirements.title: Use RESET_STREAM Final Size to carry final size
requirements.statement: The Final Size field of a RESET_STREAM frame MUST carry the final size value.

requirements.title: Account final size at connection level
requirements.statement: The receiver MUST use the final size of the stream to account for all bytes sent on the stream in its connection-level flow controller.

requirements.title: Never send data at or beyond final size
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT send data on a stream at or beyond the final size.

requirements.title: Make final size immutable once known
requirements.statement: Once a final size for a stream is known, it MUST NOT change.

requirements.title: Treat final-size changes as errors
requirements.statement: If a RESET_STREAM or STREAM frame is received indicating a change in the final size for the stream, an endpoint SHOULD respond with an error of type FINAL_SIZE_ERROR.

requirements.title: Treat data beyond final size as an error
requirements.statement: A receiver SHOULD treat receipt of data at or beyond the final size as an error of type FINAL_SIZE_ERROR, even after a stream is closed.

requirements.title: Limit the cumulative number of incoming streams
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST limit the cumulative number of incoming streams its peer can open.

requirements.title: Restrict stream IDs to the advertised limit
requirements.statement: Only streams with stream IDs less than max_streams * 4 + first_stream_id_of_type MAY be opened.

requirements.title: Set initial stream limits in transport parameters
requirements.statement: Initial limits MUST be set in the transport parameters.

requirements.title: Advertise later stream limits with MAX_STREAMS
requirements.statement: Subsequent limits MUST be advertised using MAX_STREAMS frames.

requirements.title: Apply separate limits by stream direction
requirements.statement: Separate limits MUST apply to unidirectional and bidirectional streams.

requirements.title: Close on oversized stream-count values in transport parameters
requirements.statement: If an oversized max_streams value is received in a transport parameter, the connection MUST be closed immediately with TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR.

requirements.title: Close on oversized stream-count values in frames
requirements.statement: If an oversized max_streams value is received in a frame, the connection MUST be closed immediately with FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR.

requirements.title: Do not exceed the peer's stream limit
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST NOT exceed the limit set by their peer.

requirements.title: Treat over-limit stream IDs as STREAM_LIMIT_ERROR
requirements.statement: An endpoint that receives a frame with a stream ID exceeding the limit it has sent MUST treat this as a connection error of type STREAM_LIMIT_ERROR.

requirements.title: Ignore smaller advertised stream limits
requirements.statement: Once a receiver advertises a stream limit using the MAX_STREAMS frame, advertising a smaller limit MUST have no effect.

requirements.title: Ignore non-increasing MAX_STREAMS frames
requirements.statement: MAX_STREAMS frames that do not increase the stream limit MUST be ignored.

requirements.title: Signal blocked stream openings with STREAMS_BLOCKED
requirements.statement: An endpoint that is unable to open a new stream due to the peer's limits SHOULD send a STREAMS_BLOCKED frame.

requirements.title: Do not wait for STREAMS_BLOCKED before advertising more credit
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT wait to receive STREAMS_BLOCKED before advertising additional credit.

requirements.title: Treat a QUIC connection as shared client-server state
requirements.statement: A QUIC connection MUST be shared state between a client and a server.

requirements.title: Start every connection with a handshake phase
requirements.statement: Each connection MUST start with a handshake phase during which the two endpoints establish a shared secret using the cryptographic handshake protocol and negotiate the application protocol.

requirements.title: Use the handshake to confirm willingness and establish parameters
requirements.statement: The handshake MUST confirm that both endpoints are willing to communicate and establish parameters for the connection.

requirements.title: Allow limited connection use during the handshake
requirements.statement: An application protocol MAY use the connection during the handshake phase with some limitations.

requirements.title: Allow 0-RTT client data before server response
requirements.statement: 0-RTT MUST allow application data to be sent by a client before receiving a response from the server.

requirements.title: Do not provide replay protection for 0-RTT
requirements.statement: 0-RTT MUST NOT provide protection against replay attacks.

requirements.title: Allow server data before final handshake messages
requirements.statement: A server MAY send application data to a client before it receives the final cryptographic handshake messages that allow it to confirm the identity and liveness of the client.

requirements.title: Use connection IDs to enable migration
requirements.statement: Connection IDs MUST allow connections to migrate to a new network path, both as a direct choice of an endpoint and when forced by a change in a middlebox.

requirements.title: Assign each connection a set of CIDs
requirements.statement: Each connection MUST possess a set of connection IDs, each of which can identify the connection.

requirements.title: Let endpoints choose peer-used connection IDs
requirements.statement: Connection IDs MUST be independently selected by endpoints, with each endpoint selecting the connection IDs that its peer uses.

requirements.title: Use CIDs to survive address changes
requirements.statement: Connection IDs MUST ensure that changes in lower-layer addressing do not cause packets for a QUIC connection to be delivered to the wrong endpoint.

requirements.title: Make CID selection routable and recognizable
requirements.statement: Each endpoint MUST select connection IDs using an implementation-specific method that allows packets with that connection ID to be routed back to the endpoint and to be identified by the endpoint upon receipt.

requirements.title: Use multiple CIDs for privacy
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST use multiple connection IDs so that they can send packets that an observer cannot identify as belonging to the same connection without cooperation from an endpoint.

requirements.title: Keep CIDs free of correlating information
requirements.statement: Connection IDs MUST NOT contain any information that can be used by an external observer to correlate them with other connection IDs for the same connection.

requirements.title: Do not issue the same CID twice on a connection
requirements.statement: The same connection ID MUST NOT be issued more than once on the same connection.

requirements.title: Include CID fields in long headers
requirements.statement: Packets with long headers MUST include Source Connection ID and Destination Connection ID fields.

requirements.title: Include only the destination CID in short headers
requirements.statement: Packets with short headers MUST include only the Destination Connection ID and omit the explicit length.

requirements.title: Know the short-header destination CID length
requirements.statement: The length of the Destination Connection ID field MUST be known to endpoints.

requirements.title: Allow load balancers to agree on CID handling
requirements.statement: Endpoints using a load balancer that routes based on connection ID MAY agree with the load balancer on a fixed length for connection IDs or on an encoding scheme.

requirements.title: Echo client CIDs in Version Negotiation
requirements.statement: A Version Negotiation packet MUST echo the connection IDs selected by the client.

requirements.title: Permit zero-length CIDs when routing does not need them
requirements.statement: A zero-length connection ID MAY be used when a connection ID is not needed to route to the correct endpoint.

requirements.title: Avoid concurrent zero-length-CID connections on the same address tuple
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT use the same IP address and port for multiple concurrent connections with zero-length connection IDs unless it is certain that peer connection migration, NAT rebinding, and client port reuse are not in use.

requirements.title: Maintain a supply of peer-usable connection IDs
requirements.statement: When an endpoint uses a non-zero-length connection ID, it MUST ensure that the peer has a supply of connection IDs from which to choose for packets sent to the endpoint.

requirements.title: Associate each CID with a sequence number
requirements.statement: Each connection ID MUST have an associated sequence number to assist in detecting when NEW_CONNECTION_ID or RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames refer to the same value.

requirements.title: Carry the initial CID in the long-header Source CID field
requirements.statement: The initial connection ID issued by an endpoint MUST be sent in the Source Connection ID field of the long packet header during the handshake.

requirements.title: Assign sequence number 0 to the initial CID
requirements.statement: The sequence number of the initial connection ID MUST be 0.

requirements.title: Assign sequence number 1 to the preferred-address CID
requirements.statement: If the preferred_address transport parameter is sent, the sequence number of the supplied connection ID MUST be 1.

requirements.title: Use NEW_CONNECTION_ID for additional CIDs
requirements.statement: Additional connection IDs MUST be communicated to the peer using NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames.

requirements.title: Increment CID sequence numbers by one
requirements.statement: The sequence number on each newly issued connection ID MUST increase by 1.

requirements.title: Do not sequence the first client-selected CID or Retry-provided CID
requirements.statement: The connection ID that a client selects for the first Destination Connection ID field it sends and any connection ID provided by a Retry packet MUST NOT be assigned sequence numbers.

requirements.title: Accept packets carrying an issued CID for the connection lifetime
requirements.statement: When an endpoint issues a connection ID, it MUST accept packets that carry this connection ID for the duration of the connection or until its peer invalidates the connection ID via a RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frame.

requirements.title: Treat active CIDs as valid at any time
requirements.statement: Connection IDs that are issued and not retired MUST be considered active and valid for use with the current connection at any time in any packet type.

requirements.title: Try to keep enough unused CIDs available
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD ensure that its peer has a sufficient number of available and unused connection IDs.

requirements.title: Advertise active CID limits with transport parameters
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST advertise the number of active connection IDs they are willing to maintain using the active_connection_id_limit transport parameter.

requirements.title: Do not exceed the peer's active CID limit
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT provide more connection IDs than the peer's limit.

requirements.title: Allow temporary CID excess only with immediate retirement requirements
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY send connection IDs that temporarily exceed a peer's limit if the NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame also requires the retirement of any excess by including a sufficiently large value in the Retire Prior To field.

requirements.title: Close on too many active CIDs
requirements.statement: After processing a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame and adding and retiring active connection IDs, if the number of active connection IDs exceeds the value advertised in the active_connection_id_limit transport parameter, an endpoint MUST close the connection with an error of type CONNECTION_ID_LIMIT_ERROR.

requirements.title: Supply a new CID when the peer retires one
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD supply a new connection ID when the peer retires a connection ID.

requirements.title: May replenish CIDs on previously unused packet receipt
requirements.statement: If an endpoint provided fewer connection IDs than the peer's active_connection_id_limit, it MAY supply a new connection ID when it receives a packet with a previously unused connection ID.

requirements.title: Allow limiting the total number of issued CIDs
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY limit the total number of connection IDs issued for each connection to avoid the risk of running out of connection IDs.

requirements.title: Allow limiting CID issuance to reduce per-path state
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY also limit the issuance of connection IDs to reduce the amount of per-path state it maintains.

requirements.title: Keep enough peer-usable CIDs for migration
requirements.statement: An endpoint that initiates migration and requires non-zero-length connection IDs SHOULD ensure that the pool of connection IDs available to its peer allows the peer to use a new connection ID on migration.

requirements.title: Forbid new CIDs after selecting zero-length CID in the handshake
requirements.statement: An endpoint that selects a zero-length connection ID during the handshake MUST NOT issue a new connection ID.

requirements.title: Use zero-length destination CIDs toward such endpoints
requirements.statement: A zero-length Destination Connection ID field MUST be used in all packets sent toward such an endpoint over any network path.

requirements.title: Allow switching to another available CID
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY change the connection ID it uses for a peer to another available one at any time during the connection.

requirements.title: Consume CIDs in response to migration
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY consume connection IDs in response to a migrating peer.

requirements.title: Maintain a set of peer-issued CIDs
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST maintain a set of connection IDs received from its peer for use when sending packets.

requirements.title: Retire a CID by sending RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID
requirements.statement: When the endpoint wishes to remove a connection ID from use, it MUST send a RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frame to its peer.

requirements.title: Make RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID a no-reuse request
requirements.statement: Sending a RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frame MUST indicate that the connection ID will not be used again and request that the peer replace it with a new connection ID using a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame.

requirements.title: Limit a CID to one local and one destination address
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST limit the use of a connection ID to packets sent from a single local address to a single destination address.

requirements.title: Retire CIDs when their address pair is no longer active
requirements.statement: Endpoints SHOULD retire connection IDs when they are no longer actively using either the local or destination address for which the connection ID was used.

requirements.title: Prompt peer retirement with an increased Retire Prior To
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY cause its peer to retire connection IDs by sending a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame with an increased Retire Prior To field.

requirements.title: Continue accepting retired CIDs until the peer retires them
requirements.statement: The endpoint SHOULD continue to accept the previously issued connection IDs until they are retired by the peer.

requirements.title: Allow closing when indicated CIDs can no longer be processed
requirements.statement: If the endpoint can no longer process the indicated connection IDs, it MAY close the connection.

requirements.title: Stop using CIDs after a larger Retire Prior To value
requirements.statement: Upon receipt of an increased Retire Prior To field, the peer MUST stop using the corresponding connection IDs and retire them with RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames before adding the newly provided connection ID to the set of active connection IDs.

requirements.title: Limit locally retired but unacknowledged RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD limit the number of connection IDs it has retired locally for which RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames have not yet been acknowledged.

requirements.title: Track at least twice the active CID limit
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD allow for sending and tracking a number of RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames of at least twice the value of the active_connection_id_limit transport parameter.

requirements.title: Never forget a CID without retiring it
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT forget a connection ID without retiring it.

requirements.title: Allow treating excessive unretired CIDs as an error
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY treat having connection IDs in need of retirement that exceed this limit as a connection error of type CONNECTION_ID_LIMIT_ERROR.

requirements.title: Avoid premature Retire Prior To updates
requirements.statement: Endpoints SHOULD NOT issue updates of the Retire Prior To field before receiving RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames that retire all connection IDs indicated by the previous Retire Prior To value.

requirements.title: Classify incoming packets on receipt
requirements.statement: Incoming packets MUST be classified on receipt.

requirements.title: Allow existing-connection association or new server connections
requirements.statement: Packets MAY either be associated with an existing connection or, for servers, potentially create a new connection.

requirements.title: Try to associate packets with an existing connection
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST try to associate a packet with an existing connection.

requirements.title: Process packets that match the existing CID
requirements.statement: If the packet has a non-zero-length Destination Connection ID corresponding to an existing connection, QUIC MUST process that packet accordingly.

requirements.title: Process zero-length-CID packets by address match
requirements.statement: If the Destination Connection ID is zero length and the addressing information in the packet matches the addressing information the endpoint uses to identify a connection with a zero-length connection ID, QUIC MUST process the packet as part of that connection.

requirements.title: Allow fragile zero-length-CID matching strategies
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY use just the destination IP and port or both source and destination addresses for identification.

requirements.title: Allow Stateless Reset for unattributed packets
requirements.statement: Endpoints MAY send a Stateless Reset for any packets that cannot be attributed to an existing connection.

requirements.title: Discard packets inconsistent with connection state
requirements.statement: Packets that are matched to an existing connection MUST be discarded if the packets are inconsistent with the state of that connection.

requirements.title: Discard packets with a different protocol version
requirements.statement: Packets MUST be discarded if they indicate a different protocol version than that of the connection.

requirements.title: Discard packets if packet protection cannot be removed
requirements.statement: Packets MUST be discarded if removal of packet protection is unsuccessful once the expected keys are available.

requirements.title: Allow discarding weakly protected invalid packets
requirements.statement: Invalid packets that lack strong integrity protection, such as Initial, Retry, or Version Negotiation, MAY be discarded.

requirements.title: Generate a connection error on pre-error processing
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST generate a connection error if it processes the contents of these packets before discovering an error.

requirements.title: Revert changes made while processing weakly protected invalid packets
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST fully revert any changes made during that processing.

requirements.title: Match client packets by client-selected CID
requirements.statement: Valid packets sent to clients MUST always include a Destination Connection ID that matches a value the client selects.

requirements.title: Allow zero-length CID matching by local address and port
requirements.statement: Clients that choose to receive zero-length connection IDs MAY use the local address and port to identify a connection.

requirements.title: Discard packets that do not match an existing client connection
requirements.statement: Packets that do not match an existing connection based on Destination Connection ID or, if that value is zero length, local IP address and port MUST be discarded.

requirements.title: Allow clients to drop or buffer not-yet-decryptable packets
requirements.statement: A client MAY drop packets encrypted with a key it has not yet computed or buffer them in anticipation of later packets that allow it to compute the key.

requirements.title: Discard packets with a different selected version
requirements.statement: If a client receives a packet that uses a different version than it initially selected, it MUST discard that packet.

requirements.title: Send Version Negotiation for large unsupported packets
requirements.statement: If a server receives a packet that indicates an unsupported version and the packet is large enough to initiate a new connection for any supported version, the server SHOULD send a Version Negotiation packet.

requirements.title: Allow limiting Version Negotiation responses
requirements.statement: A server MAY limit the number of packets to which it responds with a Version Negotiation packet.

requirements.title: Drop smaller unsupported-version packets
requirements.statement: Servers MUST drop smaller packets that specify unsupported versions.

requirements.title: Prefer Version Negotiation when possible
requirements.statement: Servers SHOULD respond with a Version Negotiation packet, provided that the datagram is sufficiently long.

requirements.title: Match supported-version packets by connection ID or address tuple
requirements.statement: Packets with a supported version or no Version field MUST be matched to a connection using the connection ID or, for packets with zero-length connection IDs, the local address and port.

requirements.title: Proceed with the handshake on a conforming Initial packet
requirements.statement: If the packet is an Initial packet fully conforming with the specification, the server MUST proceed with the handshake.

requirements.title: Refuse new connections with CONNECTION_REFUSED when appropriate
requirements.statement: If a server refuses to accept a new connection, it SHOULD send an Initial packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame with error code CONNECTION_REFUSED.

requirements.title: Allow limited buffering of 0-RTT packets
requirements.statement: If the packet is a 0-RTT packet, the server MAY buffer a limited number of these packets in anticipation of a late-arriving Initial packet.

requirements.title: Ignore premature Handshake packets from clients
requirements.statement: Servers SHOULD ignore any Handshake packets sent before the client receives a server response.

requirements.title: Drop all other incoming packets
requirements.statement: Servers MUST drop incoming packets under all other circumstances.

requirements.title: Allow out-of-band forwarding for simple load balancers
requirements.statement: Servers MAY use an out-of-band mechanism to forward packets to the correct server based on connection ID.

requirements.title: Allow preferred-address migration for dedicated server addresses
requirements.statement: If servers can use a dedicated server IP address or port other than the one that the client initially connects to, they MAY use the preferred_address transport parameter to request that clients move connections to that dedicated address.

requirements.title: Allow clients to decline preferred addresses
requirements.statement: Clients MAY choose not to use the preferred address.

requirements.title: Signal lack of migration support with disable_active_migration
requirements.statement: A server in a deployment that does not implement a solution to maintain connection continuity when the client address changes SHOULD indicate that migration is not supported by using the disable_active_migration transport parameter.

requirements.title: Do not let disable_active_migration block preferred-address migration
requirements.statement: The disable_active_migration transport parameter MUST NOT prohibit connection migration after a client has acted on a preferred_address transport parameter.

requirements.title: Avoid stateless reset oracles in simple load balancing
requirements.statement: Server deployments that use this simple form of load balancing MUST avoid the creation of a stateless reset oracle.

requirements.title: Allow clients to open connections
requirements.statement: When implementing the client role, an application protocol MAY open a connection, which begins the exchange described in Section 7.

requirements.title: Allow clients to enable Early Data
requirements.statement: When implementing the client role, an application protocol MAY enable Early Data when available.

requirements.title: Allow clients to learn Early Data acceptance
requirements.statement: When implementing the client role, an application protocol MAY be informed when Early Data has been accepted or rejected by a server.

requirements.title: Allow servers to listen for connections
requirements.statement: When implementing the server role, an application protocol MAY listen for incoming connections, which prepares for the exchange described in Section 7.

requirements.title: Allow servers to embed application data in resumption tickets
requirements.statement: If Early Data is supported, an application protocol MAY embed application-controlled data in the TLS resumption ticket sent to the client.

requirements.title: Allow servers to use resumption-ticket data for Early Data decisions
requirements.statement: If Early Data is supported, an application protocol MAY retrieve application-controlled data from the client's resumption ticket and accept or reject Early Data based on that information.

requirements.title: Allow configuring initial stream counts
requirements.statement: In either role, an application protocol MAY configure minimum values for the initial number of permitted streams of each type as communicated in the transport parameters.

requirements.title: Allow receive-buffer control via flow control limits
requirements.statement: In either role, an application protocol MAY control resource allocation for receive buffers by setting flow control limits both for streams and for the connection.

requirements.title: Allow handshake status queries
requirements.statement: In either role, an application protocol MAY identify whether the handshake has completed successfully or is still ongoing.

requirements.title: Allow connection liveness maintenance
requirements.statement: In either role, an application protocol MAY keep a connection from silently closing by either generating PING frames or requesting that the transport send additional frames before the idle timeout expires.

requirements.title: Allow immediate connection close
requirements.statement: In either role, an application protocol MAY immediately close the connection.

requirements.title: Size the first datagram for multi-version support
requirements.statement: Clients that support multiple QUIC versions SHOULD ensure that the first UDP datagram they send is sized to the largest of the minimum datagram sizes from all versions they support, using PADDING frames as necessary.

requirements.title: Clients that support multiple QUIC versions SHOULD ensure that the first UDP datagram they send is sized to the largest of the minimum datagram sizes from all versions they support, using PADDING frames (Section 19
requirements.statement: Clients that support multiple QUIC versions SHOULD ensure that the first UDP datagram they send is sized to the largest of the minimum datagram sizes from all versions they support, using PADDING frames (Section 19.1) as necessary.

requirements.title: Send Version Negotiation with accepted versions
requirements.statement: If the version selected by the client is not acceptable to the server, the server MUST respond with a Version Negotiation packet that includes a list of versions the server will accept.

requirements.title: Forbid Version Negotiation responses to Version Negotiation
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT send a Version Negotiation packet in response to receiving a Version Negotiation packet.

requirements.title: Limit Version Negotiation volume
requirements.statement: A server MAY limit the number of Version Negotiation packets it sends.

requirements.title: Reject unsupported Version Negotiation attempts
requirements.statement: A client that supports only this version of QUIC MUST abandon the current connection attempt if it receives a Version Negotiation packet unless it has received and successfully processed any other packet or the Version Negotiation packet lists the QUIC version selected by the client.
notes: This clause consolidates the lead rule and both exception sentences into one atomic rule.

requirements.title: That supports only this version of QUIC MUST abandon the current connection attempt if it receives a Version Negotiation packet, with the following two exceptions
requirements.statement: A client that supports only this version of QUIC MUST abandon the current connection attempt if it receives a Version Negotiation packet, with the following two exceptions.

requirements.title: Discard any Version Negotiation packet if it has received and successfully processed any other packet, including an earlier Version Negotiation packet
requirements.statement: A client MUST discard any Version Negotiation packet if it has received and successfully processed any other packet, including an earlier Version Negotiation packet.

requirements.title: Discard a Version Negotiation packet that lists the QUIC version selected by the client
requirements.statement: A client MUST discard a Version Negotiation packet that lists the QUIC version selected by the client.

requirements.title: Use reserved versions to test ignoring
requirements.statement: Endpoints MAY add reserved versions to any field where unknown or unsupported versions are ignored to test that a peer correctly ignores the value.

requirements.title: Use reserved versions to test discarding
requirements.statement: Endpoints MAY send packets with a reserved version to test that a peer correctly discards the packet.

requirements.title: Provide the following properties:
requirements.statement: The cryptographic handshake MUST provide the following properties:

requirements.title: Provide authenticated key exchange
requirements.statement: The cryptographic handshake MUST provide authenticated key exchange.

requirements.title: Always authenticate the server
requirements.statement: The cryptographic handshake MUST always authenticate the server.

requirements.title: Allow optional client authentication
requirements.statement: The cryptographic handshake MAY optionally authenticate the client.

requirements.title: Produce distinct keys per connection
requirements.statement: Every connection MUST produce distinct and unrelated keys.

requirements.title: Use handshake keys for 0-RTT and 1-RTT protection
requirements.statement: Keying material from the cryptographic handshake MUST be usable for packet protection for both 0-RTT and 1-RTT packets.

requirements.title: Authenticate transport parameter exchange
requirements.statement: The cryptographic handshake MUST provide authenticated exchange of transport parameter values for both endpoints.

requirements.title: Protect server transport parameters
requirements.statement: The cryptographic handshake MUST provide confidentiality protection for server transport parameters.

requirements.title: Authenticate application protocol negotiation
requirements.statement: The cryptographic handshake MUST provide authenticated negotiation of an application protocol.

requirements.title: Start CRYPTO frame offsets at zero
requirements.statement: The offsets used by CRYPTO frames to ensure ordered delivery of cryptographic handshake data MUST start from zero in each packet number space.

requirements.title: Explicitly negotiate an application protocol
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST explicitly negotiate an application protocol.

requirements.title: Use long-header packets to establish connection IDs during the handshake
requirements.statement: During the handshake, packets with the long header MUST be used to establish the connection IDs used by both endpoints.

requirements.title: Use Source Connection ID to select the peer-facing Destination Connection ID
requirements.statement: Each endpoint MUST use the Source Connection ID field to specify the connection ID that is used in the Destination Connection ID field of packets being sent to it.

requirements.title: Copy the peer Source Connection ID into subsequent packets
requirements.statement: After processing the first Initial packet, each endpoint MUST set the Destination Connection ID field in subsequent packets it sends to the value of the Source Connection ID field it received.

requirements.title: Use an unpredictable client Destination Connection ID
requirements.statement: When a client sends its first Initial packet without having previously received an Initial or Retry packet from the server, it MUST populate the Destination Connection ID field with an unpredictable value.

requirements.title: This Destination Connection ID MUST be at least 8 bytes in length
requirements.statement: This Destination Connection ID MUST be at least 8 bytes in length.

requirements.title: Until a packet is received from the server, the client MUST use the same Destination Connection ID value on all packets in this connection
requirements.statement: Until a packet is received from the server, the client MUST use the same Destination Connection ID value on all packets in this connection.

requirements.title: Populate the client Source Connection ID
requirements.statement: The client MUST populate the Source Connection ID field with a value of its choosing and set the Source Connection ID Length field to indicate the length.

requirements.title: Keep 0-RTT connection IDs aligned with the first Initial packet
requirements.statement: 0-RTT packets in the first flight MUST use the same Destination Connection ID and Source Connection ID values as the client's first Initial packet.

requirements.title: Adopt the server Source Connection ID on first receipt
requirements.statement: Upon first receiving an Initial or Retry packet from the server, the client MUST use the Source Connection ID supplied by the server as the Destination Connection ID for subsequent packets, including any 0-RTT packets.

requirements.title: Once a client has received a valid Initial packet from the server, it MUST discard any subsequent packet it receives on that connection with a different Source Connection ID
requirements.statement: Once a client has received a valid Initial packet from the server, it MUST discard any subsequent packet it receives on that connection with a different Source Connection ID.

requirements.title: Change the Destination Connection ID it uses for sending packets in response to only the first received Initial or Retry packet
requirements.statement: A client MUST change the Destination Connection ID it uses for sending packets in response to only the first received Initial or Retry packet.

requirements.title: Set the Destination Connection ID it uses for sending packets based on the first received Initial packet
requirements.statement: A server MUST set the Destination Connection ID it uses for sending packets based on the first received Initial packet.

requirements.title: Allow further Destination Connection ID changes only from NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY make further changes to the Destination Connection ID only if the values are taken from NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames.

requirements.title: Discard subsequent Initial packets with a different Source Connection ID
requirements.statement: Subsequent Initial packets that include a different Source Connection ID MUST be discarded.

requirements.title: Authenticate connection ID choices through transport parameters
requirements.statement: The choice each endpoint makes about connection IDs during the handshake MUST be authenticated by including all values in transport parameters.

requirements.title: Echo the client Source Connection ID in initial_source_connection_id
requirements.statement: Each endpoint MUST include the value of the Source Connection ID field from the first Initial packet it sent in the initial_source_connection_id transport parameter.

requirements.title: Echo the client Initial Destination Connection ID in original_destination_connection_id
requirements.statement: A server MUST include the Destination Connection ID field from the first Initial packet it received from the client in the original_destination_connection_id transport parameter.

requirements.title: Echo the Retry Source Connection ID when Retry is used
requirements.statement: If it sends a Retry packet, a server MUST also include the Source Connection ID field from the Retry packet in the retry_source_connection_id transport parameter.

requirements.title: The values provided by a peer for these transport parameters MUST match the values that an endpoint used in the Destination and Source Connection ID fields of Initial packets that it sent (and received, for servers)
requirements.statement: The values provided by a peer for these transport parameters MUST match the values that an endpoint used in the Destination and Source Connection ID fields of Initial packets that it sent (and received, for servers).

requirements.title: Validate that received transport parameters match received connection ID values
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST validate that received transport parameters match received connection ID values.

requirements.title: Treat the absence of the initial_source_connection_id transport parameter from either endpoint or the absence of the original_destination_connection_id transport parameter from the server as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST treat the absence of the initial_source_connection_id transport parameter from either endpoint or the absence of the original_destination_connection_id transport parameter from the server as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR.

requirements.title: Treat the following as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR or PROTOCOL_VIOLATION:
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST treat the following as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR or PROTOCOL_VIOLATION:

requirements.title: Include zero-length connection IDs as zero-length transport parameters
requirements.statement: If a zero-length connection ID is selected, the corresponding transport parameter MUST be included with a zero-length value.

requirements.title: Treat receipt of a transport parameter with an invalid value as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST treat receipt of a transport parameter with an invalid value as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR.

requirements.title: Send a parameter more than once in a given transport parameters extension
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT send a parameter more than once in a given transport parameters extension.

requirements.title: Treat receipt of duplicate transport parameters as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD treat receipt of duplicate transport parameters as a connection error of type TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR.

requirements.title: The definition of a new transport parameter (Section 7
requirements.statement: The definition of a new transport parameter (Section 7.4.2) MUST specify whether storing the transport parameter for 0-RTT is mandatory, optional, or prohibited.

requirements.title: Use remembered values for the following parameters: ack_delay_exponent, max_ack_delay, initial_source_connection_id, original_destination_connection_id, preferred_address, retry_source_connection_id, and stateless_reset_token
requirements.statement: A client MUST NOT use remembered values for the following parameters: ack_delay_exponent, max_ack_delay, initial_source_connection_id, original_destination_connection_id, preferred_address, retry_source_connection_id, and stateless_reset_token.

requirements.title: Use the server's new values in the handshake instead
requirements.statement: The client MUST use the server's new values in the handshake instead; if the server does not provide new values, the default values are used.

requirements.title: That attempts to send 0-RTT data MUST remember all other transport parameters used by the server that it is able to process
requirements.statement: A client that attempts to send 0-RTT data MUST remember all other transport parameters used by the server that it is able to process.

requirements.title: If 0-RTT data is accepted by the server, the server MUST NOT reduce any limits or alter any values that might be violated by the client with its 0-RTT data
requirements.statement: If 0-RTT data is accepted by the server, the server MUST NOT reduce any limits or alter any values that might be violated by the client with its 0-RTT data.

requirements.title: In particular, a server that accepts 0-RTT data MUST NOT set values for the following parameters (Section 18
requirements.statement: In particular, a server that accepts 0-RTT data MUST NOT set values for the following parameters (Section 18.2) that are smaller than the remembered values of the parameters.

requirements.title: The applicable subset of transport parameters that permit the sending of application data SHOULD be set to non-zero values for 0-RTT
requirements.statement: The applicable subset of transport parameters that permit the sending of application data SHOULD be set to non-zero values for 0-RTT.

requirements.title: Store and recover the previously sent values of the max_idle_timeout, max_udp_payload_size, and disable_active_migration parameters and reject 0-RTT if it selects smaller values
requirements.statement: A server MAY store and recover the previously sent values of the max_idle_timeout, max_udp_payload_size, and disable_active_migration parameters and reject 0-RTT if it selects smaller values.

requirements.title: Reject 0-RTT data if the restored values for transport parameters cannot be supported
requirements.statement: A server MUST reject 0-RTT data if the restored values for transport parameters cannot be supported.

requirements.title: Do not use updated transport-parameter values in 0-RTT
requirements.statement: When sending frames in 0-RTT packets, a client MUST NOT use updated values it learns from the server's updated transport parameters or from frames received in 1-RTT packets.

requirements.title: Use remembered 0-RTT values only until the handshake completes
requirements.statement: When sending frames in 0-RTT packets, a client MUST only use remembered transport parameters.

requirements.title: Allow 0-RTT only with remembered transport parameters
requirements.statement: Updated values of transport parameters from the handshake MUST apply only to 1-RTT packets.

requirements.title: Let updated transport-parameter values in 0-RTT signal a protocol violation
requirements.statement: A server MAY treat the use of updated transport parameters in 0-RTT as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

requirements.title: Ignore transport parameters that it does not support
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST ignore transport parameters that it does not support.

requirements.title: Support buffering at least 4096 bytes of data received in out-of-order CRYPTO frames
requirements.statement: Implementations MUST support buffering at least 4096 bytes of data received in out-of-order CRYPTO frames.

requirements.title: Choose to allow more data to be buffered during the handshake
requirements.statement: Endpoints MAY choose to allow more data to be buffered during the handshake.

requirements.title: If an endpoint does not expand its buffer, it MUST close the connection with a CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED error code
requirements.statement: If an endpoint does not expand its buffer, it MUST close the connection with a CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED error code.

requirements.title: Once the handshake completes, if an endpoint is unable to buffer all data in a CRYPTO frame, it MAY discard that CRYPTO frame and all CRYPTO frames received in the future, or it MAY close the connection with a CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED error code
requirements.statement: Once the handshake completes, if an endpoint is unable to buffer all data in a CRYPTO frame, it MAY discard that CRYPTO frame and all future CRYPTO frames or close the connection with a CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED error code.

requirements.title: Containing discarded CRYPTO frames MUST be acknowledged because the packet has been received and processed by the transport even though the CRYPTO frame was discarded
requirements.statement: Packets containing discarded CRYPTO frames MUST be acknowledged because the packet has been received and processed by the transport even though the CRYPTO frame was discarded.

requirements.title: Limit sending to unvalidated addresses
requirements.statement: After receiving packets from an address that is not yet validated, an endpoint MUST limit the amount of data it sends to the unvalidated address to three times the amount of data received from that address.
notes: The source sentence begins with explanatory lead-in text. This canonical clause retains the atomic obligation, while [`REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P1-0002`](./SPEC-QUIC-RFC9000.json) preserves the cleaner section-scoped restatement from RFC 9000 Section 8.1.

requirements.title: Additionally, an endpoint MAY consider the peer address validated if the peer uses a connection ID chosen by the endpoint and the connection ID contains at least 64 bits of entropy
requirements.statement: Additionally, an endpoint MAY consider the peer address validated if the peer uses a connection ID chosen by the endpoint and the connection ID contains at least 64 bits of entropy.

requirements.title: Prior to validating the client address, servers MUST NOT send more than three times as many bytes as the number of bytes they have received
requirements.statement: Prior to validating the client address, servers MUST NOT send more than three times as many bytes as the number of bytes they have received.

requirements.title: For the purposes of avoiding amplification prior to address validation, servers MUST count all of the payload bytes received in datagrams that are uniquely attributed to a single connection
requirements.statement: For the purposes of avoiding amplification prior to address validation, servers MUST count all of the payload bytes received in datagrams that are uniquely attributed to a single connection.

requirements.title: Clients MUST ensure that UDP datagrams containing Initial packets have UDP payloads of at least 1200 bytes, adding PADDING frames as necessary
requirements.statement: Clients MUST ensure that UDP datagrams containing Initial packets have UDP payloads of at least 1200 bytes, adding PADDING frames as necessary.

requirements.title: To prevent this deadlock, clients MUST send a packet on a Probe Timeout (PTO)
requirements.statement: To prevent this deadlock, clients MUST send a packet on a Probe Timeout (PTO); see Section 6.2 of [QUIC-RECOVERY].

requirements.title: Specifically, the client MUST send an Initial packet in a UDP datagram that contains at least 1200 bytes if it does not have Handshake keys, and otherwise send a Handshake packet
requirements.statement: Specifically, the client MUST send an Initial packet in a UDP datagram that contains at least 1200 bytes if it does not have Handshake keys, and otherwise send a Handshake packet.

requirements.title: Probe on PTO to avoid handshake deadlock
requirements.statement: To prevent deadlock, clients MUST send a packet on a Probe Timeout (PTO).

requirements.title: Sent in a NEW_TOKEN frame or a Retry packet MUST be constructed in a way that allows the server to identify how it was provided to a client
requirements.statement: A token sent in a NEW_TOKEN frame or a Retry packet MUST be constructed in a way that allows the server to identify how it was provided to a client.

requirements.title: This token MUST be repeated by the client in all Initial packets it sends for that connection after it receives the Retry packet
requirements.statement: This token MUST be repeated by the client in all Initial packets it sends for that connection after it receives the Retry packet.

requirements.title: Instead, the server SHOULD immediately close (Section 10
requirements.statement: Instead, the server SHOULD immediately close (Section 10.2) the connection with an INVALID_TOKEN error.

requirements.title: Validate Retry token returns
requirements.statement: The Retry token MUST be repeated by the client in all Initial packets it sends for that connection after it receives the Retry packet.

requirements.title: Close on invalid Retry tokens
