requirements.statement: If a server receives a client Initial that contains an invalid Retry token but is otherwise valid, it SHOULD immediately close the connection with an INVALID_TOKEN error.

requirements.title: Provide clients with an address validation token during one connection that can be used on a subsequent connection
requirements.statement: A server MAY provide clients with an address validation token during one connection that can be used on a subsequent connection.

requirements.title: Include the token in all Initial packets it sends, unless a Retry replaces the token with a newer one
requirements.statement: The client MUST include the token in all Initial packets it sends, unless a Retry replaces the token with a newer one.

requirements.title: Use the token provided in a Retry for future connections
requirements.statement: The client MUST NOT use the token provided in a Retry for future connections.

requirements.title: Servers MAY discard any Initial packet that does not carry the expected token
requirements.statement: Servers MAY discard any Initial packet that does not carry the expected token.

requirements.title: Thus, a token SHOULD have an expiration time, which could be either an explicit expiration time or an issued timestamp that can be used to dynamically calculate the expiration time
requirements.statement: Thus, a token SHOULD have an expiration time, which could be either an explicit expiration time or an issued timestamp that can be used to dynamically calculate the expiration time.

requirements.title: Issued with NEW_TOKEN MUST NOT include information that would allow values to be linked by an observer to the connection on which it was issued
requirements.statement: A token issued with NEW_TOKEN MUST NOT include information that would allow values to be linked by an observer to the connection on which it was issued.

requirements.title: Ensure that every NEW_TOKEN frame it sends is unique across all clients, with the exception of those sent to repair losses of previously sent NEW_TOKEN frames
requirements.statement: A server MUST ensure that every NEW_TOKEN frame it sends is unique across all clients, with the exception of those sent to repair losses of previously sent NEW_TOKEN frames.

requirements.title: Information that allows the server to distinguish between tokens from Retry and NEW_TOKEN MAY be accessible to entities other than the server
requirements.statement: Information that allows the server to distinguish between tokens from Retry and NEW_TOKEN MAY be accessible to entities other than the server.

requirements.title: When connecting to a server for which the client retains an applicable and unused token, it SHOULD include that token in the Token field of its Initial packet
requirements.statement: When connecting to a server for which the client retains an applicable and unused token, it SHOULD include that token in the Token field of its Initial packet.

requirements.title: Include a token that is not applicable to the server that it is connecting to, unless the client has the knowledge that the server that issued the token and the server the client is connecting to are jointly managing the tokens
requirements.statement: A client MUST NOT include a token that is not applicable to the server that it is connecting to, unless the client has the knowledge that the server that issued the token and the server the client is connecting to are jointly managing the tokens.

requirements.title: Use a token from any previous connection to that server
requirements.statement: A client MAY use a token from any previous connection to that server.

requirements.title: In comparison, a token obtained in a Retry packet MUST be used immediately during the connection attempt and cannot be used in subsequent connection attempts
requirements.statement: In comparison, a token obtained in a Retry packet MUST be used immediately during the connection attempt and cannot be used in subsequent connection attempts.

requirements.title: Reuse a token from a NEW_TOKEN frame for different connection attempts
requirements.statement: A client SHOULD NOT reuse a token from a NEW_TOKEN frame for different connection attempts.

requirements.title: When a server receives an Initial packet with an address validation token, it MUST attempt to validate the token, unless it has already completed address validation
requirements.statement: When a server receives an Initial packet with an address validation token, it MUST attempt to validate the token, unless it has already completed address validation.

requirements.title: If the token is invalid, then the server SHOULD proceed as if the client did not have a validated address, including potentially sending a Retry packet
requirements.statement: If the token is invalid, then the server SHOULD proceed as if the client did not have a validated address, including potentially sending a Retry packet.

requirements.title: If the validation succeeds, the server SHOULD then allow the handshake to proceed
requirements.statement: If the validation succeeds, the server SHOULD then allow the handshake to proceed.

requirements.title: Clients MAY use tokens obtained on one connection for any connection attempt using the same version
requirements.statement: Clients MAY use tokens obtained on one connection for any connection attempt using the same version.

requirements.title: An address validation token MUST be difficult to guess
requirements.statement: An address validation token MUST be difficult to guess.

requirements.title: For this design to work, the token MUST be covered by integrity protection against modification or falsification by clients
requirements.statement: For this design to work, the token MUST be covered by integrity protection against modification or falsification by clients.

requirements.title: Tokens sent in Retry packets SHOULD include information that allows the server to verify that the source IP address and port in client packets remain constant
requirements.statement: Tokens sent in Retry packets SHOULD include information that allows the server to verify that the source IP address and port in client packets remain constant.

requirements.title: Tokens sent in NEW_TOKEN frames MUST include information that allows the server to verify that the client IP address has not changed from when the token was issued
requirements.statement: Tokens sent in NEW_TOKEN frames MUST include information that allows the server to verify that the client IP address has not changed from when the token was issued.

requirements.title: If the client IP address has changed, the server MUST adhere to the anti-amplification limit
requirements.statement: If the client IP address has changed, the server MUST adhere to the anti-amplification limit; see Section 8.

requirements.title: To protect against such attacks, servers MUST ensure that replay of tokens is prevented or limited
requirements.statement: To protect against such attacks, servers MUST ensure that replay of tokens is prevented or limited.

requirements.title: Servers SHOULD ensure that tokens sent in Retry packets are only accepted for a short time, as they are returned immediately by clients
requirements.statement: Servers SHOULD ensure that tokens sent in Retry packets are only accepted for a short time, as they are returned immediately by clients.

requirements.title: Tokens that are provided in NEW_TOKEN frames (Section 19
requirements.statement: Tokens that are provided in NEW_TOKEN frames (Section 19.7) need to be valid for longer but SHOULD NOT be accepted multiple times.

requirements.title: Servers are encouraged to allow tokens to be used only once, if possible
requirements.statement: Servers are encouraged to allow tokens to be used only once, if possible; tokens MAY include additional information about clients to further narrow applicability or reuse.

requirements.title: Make address-validation tokens hard to guess and integrity protected
requirements.statement: An address validation token MUST be difficult to guess and, for token-based schemes, covered by integrity protection against modification or falsification by clients.

requirements.title: Preserve anti-amplification if the client address changes
requirements.statement: If the client IP address has changed, the server MUST adhere to the anti-amplification limit.

requirements.title: Protect against token replay
requirements.statement: To protect against replay attacks, servers MUST ensure that replay of tokens is prevented or limited.

requirements.title: Include other frames with the PATH_CHALLENGE and PATH_RESPONSE frames used for path validation
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY include other frames with the PATH_CHALLENGE and PATH_RESPONSE frames used for path validation.

requirements.title: Send multiple PATH_CHALLENGE frames to guard against packet loss
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY send multiple PATH_CHALLENGE frames to guard against packet loss.

requirements.title: However, an endpoint SHOULD NOT send multiple PATH_CHALLENGE frames in a single packet
requirements.statement: However, an endpoint SHOULD NOT send multiple PATH_CHALLENGE frames in a single packet.

requirements.title: Probe a new path with packets containing a PATH_CHALLENGE frame more frequently than it would send an Initial packet
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD NOT probe a new path with packets containing a PATH_CHALLENGE frame more frequently than it would send an Initial packet.

requirements.title: Use unpredictable data in every PATH_CHALLENGE frame so that it can associate the peer's response with the corresponding PATH_CHALLENGE
requirements.statement: The endpoint MUST use unpredictable data in every PATH_CHALLENGE frame so that it can associate the peer's response with the corresponding PATH_CHALLENGE.

requirements.title: Expand datagrams that contain a PATH_CHALLENGE frame to at least the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes, unless the anti-amplification limit for the path does not permit sending a datagram of this size
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST expand datagrams that contain a PATH_CHALLENGE frame to at least the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes, unless the anti-amplification limit for the path does not permit sending a datagram of this size.

requirements.title: To ensure that the path MTU is large enough, the endpoint MUST perform a second path validation by sending a PATH_CHALLENGE frame in a datagram of at least 1200 bytes
requirements.statement: To ensure that the path MTU is large enough, the endpoint MUST perform a second path validation by sending a PATH_CHALLENGE frame in a datagram of at least 1200 bytes.

requirements.title: Unlike other cases where datagrams are expanded, endpoints MUST NOT discard datagrams that appear to be too small when they contain PATH_CHALLENGE or PATH_RESPONSE
requirements.statement: Unlike other cases where datagrams are expanded, endpoints MUST NOT discard datagrams that appear to be too small when they contain PATH_CHALLENGE or PATH_RESPONSE.

requirements.title: Use PATH_CHALLENGE to validate a path
requirements.statement: To initiate path validation, an endpoint MUST send a PATH_CHALLENGE frame containing an unpredictable payload on the path to be validated.

requirements.title: On receiving a PATH_CHALLENGE frame, an endpoint MUST respond by echoing the data contained in the PATH_CHALLENGE frame in a PATH_RESPONSE frame
requirements.statement: On receiving a PATH_CHALLENGE frame, an endpoint MUST respond by echoing the data contained in the PATH_CHALLENGE frame in a PATH_RESPONSE frame.

requirements.title: Delay transmission of a packet containing a PATH_RESPONSE frame unless constrained by congestion control
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT delay transmission of a packet containing a PATH_RESPONSE frame unless constrained by congestion control.

requirements.title: Be sent on the network path where the PATH_CHALLENGE frame was received
requirements.statement: A PATH_RESPONSE frame MUST be sent on the network path where the PATH_CHALLENGE frame was received.

requirements.title: This requirement MUST NOT be enforced by the endpoint that initiates path validation, as that would enable an attack on migration
requirements.statement: This requirement MUST NOT be enforced by the endpoint that initiates path validation, as that would enable an attack on migration; see Section 9.3.3.

requirements.title: Expand datagrams that contain a PATH_RESPONSE frame to at least the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST expand datagrams that contain a PATH_RESPONSE frame to at least the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes.

requirements.title: However, an endpoint MUST NOT expand the datagram containing the PATH_RESPONSE if the resulting data exceeds the anti-amplification limit
requirements.statement: However, an endpoint MUST NOT expand the datagram containing the PATH_RESPONSE if the resulting data exceeds the anti-amplification limit.

requirements.title: Send more than one PATH_RESPONSE frame in response to one PATH_CHALLENGE frame
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT send more than one PATH_RESPONSE frame in response to one PATH_CHALLENGE frame; see Section 13.3.

requirements.title: Limit PATH_RESPONSE fan-out
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT send more than one PATH_RESPONSE frame in response to one PATH_CHALLENGE frame.

requirements.title: However, the endpoint MUST initiate another path validation with an expanded datagram to verify that the path supports the required MTU
requirements.statement: However, the endpoint MUST initiate another path validation with an expanded datagram to verify that the path supports the required MTU.

requirements.title: Abandon path validation based on a timer
requirements.statement: Endpoints SHOULD abandon path validation based on a timer.

requirements.title: That has no valid network path to its peer MAY signal this using the NO_VIABLE_PATH connection error, noting that this is only possible if the network path exists but does not support the required MTU (Section 14)
requirements.statement: An endpoint that has no valid network path to its peer MAY signal this using the NO_VIABLE_PATH connection error, noting that this is only possible if the network path exists but does not support the required MTU (Section 14).

requirements.title: Signal no viable path when appropriate
requirements.statement: An endpoint that has no valid network path to its peer MAY signal this using the NO_VIABLE_PATH connection error.

requirements.title: Initiate connection migration before the handshake is confirmed, as defined in Section 4
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT initiate connection migration before the handshake is confirmed, as defined in Section 4.1.2 of [QUIC-TLS].

requirements.title: If the peer sent the disable_active_migration transport parameter, an endpoint also MUST NOT send packets (including probing packets
requirements.statement: If the peer sent the disable_active_migration transport parameter, an endpoint also MUST NOT send packets (including probing packets; see Section 9.1) from a different local address to the address the peer used during the handshake, unless the endpoint has acted on a preferred_address transport parameter from the peer.

requirements.title: If the peer violates this requirement, the endpoint MUST either drop the incoming packets on that path without generating a Stateless Reset or proceed with path validation and allow the peer to migrate
requirements.statement: If the peer violates this requirement, the endpoint MUST either drop the incoming packets on that path without generating a Stateless Reset or proceed with path validation and allow the peer to migrate.

requirements.title: Perform path validation (Section 8
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST perform path validation (Section 8.2) if it detects any change to a peer's address, unless it has previously validated that address.

requirements.title: When an endpoint has no validated path on which to send packets, it MAY discard connection state
requirements.statement: When an endpoint has no validated path on which to send packets, it MAY discard connection state.

requirements.title: Capable of connection migration MAY wait for a new path to become available before discarding connection state
requirements.statement: An endpoint capable of connection migration MAY wait for a new path to become available before discarding connection state.

requirements.title: If a client receives packets from an unknown server address, the client MUST discard these packets
requirements.statement: If a client receives packets from an unknown server address, the client MUST discard these packets.

requirements.title: Forbid migration before handshake confirmation
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT initiate connection migration before the handshake is confirmed.

requirements.title: Respect Disable Active Migration
requirements.statement: If the peer sent the disable_active_migration transport parameter, an endpoint MUST NOT send packets from a different local address to the address the peer used during the handshake unless the endpoint has acted on a preferred_address transport parameter from the peer.

requirements.title: Validate Peer Address Changes
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST perform path validation if it detects any change to a peer's address unless it has previously validated that address.

requirements.title: Restrict Non-Probing Packets Until Peer Reachability Is Established
requirements.statement: Servers MUST NOT send non-probing packets toward a client address until they see a non-probing packet from that address.

requirements.title: Discard Packets from Unknown Server Addresses
requirements.statement: If a client receives packets from an unknown server address, the client MUST discard those packets.

requirements.title: Probe for peer reachability from a new local address using path validation (Section 8
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY probe for peer reachability from a new local address using path validation (Section 8.2) prior to migrating the connection to the new local address.

requirements.title: Probe New Local Addresses Before Migration
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY probe for peer reachability from a new local address using path validation before migrating the connection to the new local address.

requirements.title: Defer path validation until after a peer sends the next non-probing frame to its new address
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY defer path validation until after a peer sends the next non-probing frame to its new address.

requirements.title: Initiate Path Validation on the New Path
requirements.statement: To establish reachability on the new path, an endpoint MUST initiate path validation on the new path.

requirements.title: Reset Congestion State on Migration
requirements.statement: When migrating, an endpoint MUST immediately reset its congestion controller and round-trip time estimator for the new path to initial values unless the only change in the peer's address is its port number.

requirements.title: Retain State for Port-Only Changes
requirements.statement: When the only change in the peer's address is its port number, an endpoint MAY retain its congestion control state and round-trip estimate instead of reverting to initial values.

requirements.title: Validate ECN Capability After Migration
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST validate ECN capability as described in Section 13.4 when it migrates to a new path.

requirements.title: If the recipient permits the migration, it MUST send subsequent packets to the new peer address and MUST initiate path validation (Section 8
requirements.statement: If the recipient permits the migration, it MUST send subsequent packets to the new peer address and initiate path validation (Section 8.2) to verify the peer's ownership of the address if validation is not already underway.

requirements.title: Allow data on an unvalidated peer address
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY send data to an unvalidated peer address.

requirements.title: Protect unvalidated peer-address traffic
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST protect against potential attacks as described in Sections 9.3.1 and 9.3.2 when it sends data to an unvalidated peer address.

requirements.title: Skip validation of a peer address if that address has been seen recently
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY skip validation of a peer address if that address has been seen recently.

requirements.title: After verifying a new client address, the server SHOULD send new address validation tokens (Section 8) to the client
requirements.statement: After verifying a new client address, the server SHOULD send new address validation tokens (Section 8) to the client.

requirements.title: Send Subsequent Packets to the Migrated Address
requirements.statement: If the recipient permits the migration, it MUST send subsequent packets to the new peer address.

requirements.title: Initiate Path Validation After Permitting Migration
requirements.statement: If the recipient permits the migration, it MUST initiate path validation to verify the peer's ownership of the address if validation is not already underway.

requirements.title: Change Sending Address Only on the Highest-Numbered Non-Probing Packet
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST only change the address to which it sends packets in response to the highest-numbered non-probing packet.

requirements.title: Protect Against Address-Spoofing Attacks
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST protect against potential attacks as described in Sections 9.3.1 and 9.3.2.

requirements.title: Allow Abandoning Other Path Validation
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY abandon any path validation for other addresses after changing the address to which it sends non-probing packets.

requirements.title: Send New Address Validation Tokens
requirements.statement: After verifying a new client address, the server SHOULD send new address validation tokens to the client.

requirements.title: Limit Sending Until Address Validation
requirements.statement: Until a peer's address is deemed valid, an endpoint MUST limit the amount of data it sends to that address.

requirements.title: To protect the connection from failing due to such a spurious migration, an endpoint MUST revert to using the last validated peer address when validation of a new peer address fails
requirements.statement: To protect the connection from failing due to such a spurious migration, an endpoint MUST revert to using the last validated peer address when validation of a new peer address fails.

requirements.title: If an endpoint has no state about the last validated peer address, it MUST close the connection silently by discarding all connection state
requirements.statement: If an endpoint has no state about the last validated peer address, it MUST close the connection silently by discarding all connection state.

requirements.title: For instance, an endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to any further incoming packets
requirements.statement: For instance, an endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to any further incoming packets.

requirements.title: Revert to the Last Validated Peer Address
requirements.statement: When validation of a new peer address fails, an endpoint MUST revert to using the last validated peer address.

requirements.title: Allow Stateless Reset After Silent Close
requirements.statement: After discarding all connection state, an endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to any further incoming packets.

requirements.title: In response to an apparent migration, endpoints MUST validate the previously active path using a PATH_CHALLENGE frame
requirements.statement: In response to an apparent migration, endpoints MUST validate the previously active path using a PATH_CHALLENGE frame.

requirements.title: That receives a PATH_CHALLENGE on an active path SHOULD send a non-probing packet in response
requirements.statement: An endpoint that receives a PATH_CHALLENGE on an active path SHOULD send a non-probing packet in response.

requirements.title: Return to the Original Path After a New Highest Packet Number
requirements.statement: A non-probing packet received on the original path that increases the maximum received packet number MUST cause the endpoint to move back to that path.

requirements.title: Use Heuristics for Attack Detection
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY use heuristics to improve detection of this style of attack.

requirements.title: Sent on the old path MUST NOT contribute to congestion control or RTT estimation for the new path
requirements.statement: Packets sent on the old path MUST NOT contribute to congestion control or RTT estimation for the new path.

requirements.title: Use updated congestion state only after confirming the peer address
requirements.statement: On confirming a peer's ownership of its new address, an endpoint MUST immediately reset the congestion controller and round-trip time estimator for the new path to initial values unless the only change in the peer's address is its port number.

requirements.title: Retain congestion state for port-only changes
requirements.statement: Because port-only changes are commonly the result of NAT rebinding or other middlebox activity, the endpoint MAY instead retain its congestion control state and round-trip estimate in those cases instead of reverting to initial values.

requirements.title: Keep ACK coverage across multiple paths
requirements.statement: A receiver of packets on multiple paths MUST still send ACK frames covering all received packets.

requirements.title: Retain State for Port-Only Peer Changes
requirements.statement: When the only change in the peer's address is its port number, an endpoint MAY retain its congestion control state and round-trip estimate instead of reverting to initial values.

requirements.title: ACK All Received Packets Across Paths
requirements.statement: A receiver of packets on multiple paths MUST send ACK frames covering all received packets.

requirements.title: Allow Probe-Packet Loss Exceptions
requirements.statement: A sender MAY make exceptions for probe packets so their loss detection is independent and does not unduly cause the congestion controller to reduce its sending rate.

requirements.title: Allow a Separate PATH_CHALLENGE Timer
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY set a separate timer when a PATH_CHALLENGE is sent, and the timer is canceled if the corresponding PATH_RESPONSE is received.

requirements.title: Allow Retrying PATH_CHALLENGE on Timeout
requirements.statement: If the timer fires before the PATH_RESPONSE is received, an endpoint MAY send a new PATH_CHALLENGE and restart the timer for a longer period of time.

requirements.title: Set the PATH_CHALLENGE Timer Conservatively
requirements.statement: This timer SHOULD be set as described in Section 6.2.1 of [QUIC-RECOVERY].

requirements.title: Do Not Make the PATH_CHALLENGE Timer More Aggressive
requirements.statement: This timer MUST NOT be more aggressive.

requirements.title: At any time, endpoints MAY change the Destination Connection ID they transmit with to a value that has not been used on another path
requirements.statement: At any time, endpoints MAY change the Destination Connection ID they transmit with to a value that has not been used on another path.

requirements.title: Prevent connection-id reuse across local addresses
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT reuse a connection ID when sending from more than one local address.

requirements.title: Prevent connection-id reuse across destination addresses
requirements.statement: Similarly, an endpoint MUST NOT reuse a connection ID when sending to more than one destination address.

requirements.title: Due to network changes outside the control of its peer, an endpoint might receive packets from a new source address with the same Destination Connection ID field value, in which case it MAY continue to use the current connection ID with the new remote address while still sending from the same local address
requirements.statement: Due to network changes outside the control of its peer, an endpoint might receive packets from a new source address with the same Destination Connection ID field value, in which case it MAY continue to use the current connection ID with the new remote address while still sending from the same local address.

requirements.title: Limit use of the zero-length connection ID for migration
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD NOT initiate migration with a peer that has requested a zero-length connection ID, because traffic over the new path might be trivially linkable to traffic over the old one.

requirements.title: Keep address changes infrequent
requirements.statement: Changing address can cause a peer to reset its congestion control state, so addresses SHOULD only be changed infrequently.

requirements.title: Provide new connection IDs before peers migrate
requirements.statement: To ensure that migration is possible and packets sent on different paths cannot be correlated, endpoints SHOULD provide new connection IDs before peers migrate.

requirements.title: Keep Provided Connection IDs Unlinkable
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST ensure that connection IDs they provide cannot be linked by any other entity.

requirements.title: Allow Reuse with the Same Local Address
requirements.statement: If an endpoint receives packets from a new source address with the same Destination Connection ID field value, it MAY continue to use the current connection ID with the new remote address while still sending from the same local address.

requirements.title: Avoid Migration with Zero-Length Connection IDs
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD NOT initiate migration with a peer that has requested a zero-length connection ID.

requirements.title: Change Addresses Infrequently
requirements.statement: Addresses SHOULD only be changed infrequently.

requirements.title: Provide New Connection IDs Before Migration
requirements.statement: Endpoints SHOULD provide new connection IDs before peers migrate.

requirements.title: If a client receives packets from a new server address when the client has not initiated a migration to that address, the client SHOULD discard these packets
requirements.statement: If a client receives packets from a new server address when the client has not initiated a migration to that address, the client SHOULD discard these packets.

requirements.title: Discard Unexpected Server-Address Packets
requirements.statement: If a client receives packets from a new server address when it has not initiated a migration to that address, the client SHOULD discard those packets.

requirements.title: Servers MAY communicate a preferred address of each address family (IPv4 and IPv6) to allow clients to pick the one most suited to their network attachment
requirements.statement: Servers MAY communicate a preferred address of each address family (IPv4 and IPv6) to allow clients to pick the one most suited to their network attachment.

requirements.title: Once the handshake is confirmed, the client SHOULD select one of the two addresses provided by the server and initiate path validation (see Section 8
requirements.statement: Once the handshake is confirmed, the client SHOULD select one of the two addresses provided by the server and initiate path validation (see Section 8.2).

requirements.title: Begin sending packets to the new server address after successful validation
requirements.statement: As soon as path validation succeeds, the client SHOULD begin sending all future packets to the new server address using the new connection ID.

requirements.title: Discontinue use of the old server address after successful validation
requirements.statement: As soon as path validation succeeds, the client SHOULD discontinue use of the old server address.

requirements.title: Keep using the original server address after failed validation
requirements.statement: If path validation fails, the client MUST continue sending all future packets to the server's original IP address.

requirements.title: Convey a Preferred Address in the TLS Handshake
requirements.statement: A server MUST convey a preferred address by including the preferred_address transport parameter in the TLS handshake.

requirements.title: Allow Per-Family Preferred Addresses
requirements.statement: Servers MAY communicate a preferred address of each address family to allow clients to pick the one most suited to their network attachment.

requirements.title: Choose a Preferred Address After Handshake Confirmation
requirements.statement: Once the handshake is confirmed, the client SHOULD select one of the two addresses provided by the server and initiate path validation.

requirements.title: Use an Unused Active Connection ID for Preferred-Address Migration
requirements.statement: A client MUST construct packets using any previously unused active connection ID, taken from either the preferred_address transport parameter or a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame.

requirements.title: Move Future Packets to the New Server Address After Validation
requirements.statement: As soon as path validation succeeds, the client SHOULD begin sending all future packets to the new server address using the new connection ID and discontinue use of the old server address.

requirements.title: That migrates to a preferred address MUST validate the address it chooses before migrating
requirements.statement: A client that migrates to a preferred address MUST validate the address it chooses before migrating; see Section 21.5.3.

requirements.title: Send non-probing packets from its original address until it receives a non-probing packet from the client at its preferred address and until the server has validated the new path
requirements.statement: The server MUST send non-probing packets from its original address until it receives a non-probing packet from the client at its preferred address and until the server has validated the new path.

requirements.title: Probe on the path toward the client from its preferred address
requirements.statement: The server MUST probe on the path toward the client from its preferred address.

requirements.title: Drop newer packets for this connection that are received on the old IP address
requirements.statement: The server SHOULD drop newer packets for this connection that are received on the old IP address.

requirements.title: Continue to process delayed packets that are received on the old IP address
requirements.statement: The server MAY continue to process delayed packets that are received on the old IP address.

requirements.title: Do not use preferred-address values for other connections
requirements.statement: The addresses that a server provides in the preferred_address transport parameter MUST be treated as valid only for the connection in which they are provided.

requirements.title: Use these for other connections, including connections that are resumed from the current connection
requirements.statement: A client MUST NOT use these for other connections, including connections that are resumed from the current connection.

requirements.title: Validate the Chosen Preferred Address Before Migrating
requirements.statement: A client that migrates to a preferred address MUST validate the address it chooses before migrating.

requirements.title: Respond to PATH_CHALLENGE with PATH_RESPONSE
requirements.statement: If the received packet contains a PATH_CHALLENGE frame, the server MUST send a packet containing a PATH_RESPONSE frame as described in Section 8.2.

requirements.title: Move Exclusively to the Preferred Address After Validation
requirements.statement: Once the server has completed path validation and has received a non-probing packet with a new largest packet number on its preferred address, the server MUST send non-probing packets to the client exclusively from its preferred IP address.

requirements.title: Restrict Preferred-Address Values to the Current Connection
requirements.statement: A client MUST NOT use the addresses provided in the preferred_address transport parameter for other connections, including connections that are resumed from the current connection.

requirements.title: In this case, the client SHOULD perform path validation to both the original and preferred server address from the client's new address concurrently
requirements.statement: In this case, the client SHOULD perform path validation to both the original and preferred server address from the client's new address concurrently.

requirements.title: If path validation of the server's preferred address succeeds, the client MUST abandon validation of the original address and migrate to using the server's preferred address
requirements.statement: If path validation of the server's preferred address succeeds, the client MUST abandon validation of the original address and migrate to using the server's preferred address.

requirements.title: If path validation of the server's preferred address fails but validation of the server's original address succeeds, the client MAY migrate to its new address and continue sending to the server's original address
requirements.statement: If path validation of the server's preferred address fails but validation of the server's original address succeeds, the client MAY migrate to its new address and continue sending to the server's original address.

requirements.title: If packets received at the server's preferred address have a different source address than observed from the client during the handshake, the server MUST protect against potential attacks as described in Sections 9
requirements.statement: If packets received at the server's preferred address have a different source address than observed from the client during the handshake, the server MUST protect against potential attacks as described in Sections 9.3.1 and 9.3.2.

requirements.title: Servers SHOULD initiate path validation to the client's new address upon receiving a probe packet from a different address
requirements.statement: Servers SHOULD initiate path validation to the client's new address upon receiving a probe packet from a different address; see Section 8.

requirements.title: That migrates to a new address SHOULD use a preferred address from the same address family for the server
requirements.statement: A client that migrates to a new address SHOULD use a preferred address from the same address family for the server.

requirements.title: Keep preferred-address connection IDs path-agnostic
requirements.statement: The connection ID provided in the `preferred_address` transport parameter MAY be used independently of the addresses that are provided.

requirements.title: Use the preferred-address connection ID on any path
requirements.statement: The connection ID is provided to ensure that the client has a connection ID available for migration, but the client MAY use this connection ID on any path.

requirements.title: Protect Preferred-Address Traffic from Attacks
requirements.statement: If packets received at the server's preferred address have a different source address than was observed from the client during the handshake, the server MUST protect against potential attacks as described in Sections 9.3.1 and 9.3.2.

requirements.title: Validate the Client's New Address on Probe Packets
requirements.statement: Servers SHOULD initiate path validation to the client's new address upon receiving a probe packet from a different address.

requirements.title: Allow the Preferred-Address Connection ID on Any Path
requirements.statement: The client MAY use the connection ID provided in the preferred_address transport parameter on any path.

requirements.title: That send data using IPv6 SHOULD apply an IPv6 flow label in compliance with [RFC6437], unless the local API does not allow setting IPv6 flow labels
requirements.statement: Endpoints that send data using IPv6 SHOULD apply an IPv6 flow label in compliance with [RFC6437], unless the local API does not allow setting IPv6 flow labels.

requirements.title: The flow label generation MUST be designed to minimize the chances of linkability with a previously used flow label, as a stable flow label would enable correlating activity on multiple paths
requirements.statement: The flow label generation MUST be designed to minimize the chances of linkability with a previously used flow label, as a stable flow label would enable correlating activity on multiple paths; see Section 9.5.

requirements.title: Apply IPv6 Flow Labels
requirements.statement: Endpoints that send data using IPv6 SHOULD apply an IPv6 flow label in compliance with [RFC6437] unless the local API does not allow setting IPv6 flow labels.

requirements.title: Minimize Flow-Label Linkability
requirements.statement: The flow label generation MUST be designed to minimize the chances of linkability with a previously used flow label.

requirements.title: Discard connection state if it does not have a validated path on which it can send packets
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY discard connection state if it does not have a validated path on which it can send packets; see Section 8.2.

requirements.title: Permit discarding unvalidated state
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY discard connection state if it does not have a validated path on which it can send packets.
notes: This is an option of last resort.

requirements.title: To avoid excessively small idle timeout periods, endpoints MUST increase the idle timeout period to be at least three times the current Probe Timeout (PTO)
requirements.statement: To avoid excessively small idle timeout periods, endpoints MUST increase the idle timeout period to be at least three times the current Probe Timeout (PTO).

requirements.title: Silently close on idle timeout
requirements.statement: If a max_idle_timeout is specified by either endpoint in its transport parameters, the connection MUST be silently closed and its state discarded when it remains idle for longer than the minimum of the max_idle_timeout values advertised by both endpoints.

requirements.title: Advertise and compute idle timeout
requirements.statement: Each endpoint MUST advertise a `max_idle_timeout`, and the effective value at an endpoint is the minimum of the two advertised values, or the sole advertised value if only one endpoint advertises a non-zero value.

requirements.title: Commit to immediate close before timeout
requirements.statement: An endpoint that announces a max_idle_timeout MUST initiate an immediate close if it abandons the connection before the effective timeout expires.

requirements.title: Restart idle timer on received traffic
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST restart its idle timer when a packet from its peer is received and processed successfully.

requirements.title: Restart idle timer on fresh ack-eliciting send
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST also restart its idle timer when it sends an ack-eliciting packet if no other ack-eliciting packets have been sent since it last received and processed a packet.

requirements.title: Bound idle timeout by PTO
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST increase the idle timeout period to be at least three times the current Probe Timeout (PTO).

requirements.title: Allow liveness probes
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY send a PING or another ack-eliciting frame to test the connection for liveness if the peer could time out soon.

requirements.title: Application protocols that use QUIC SHOULD provide guidance on when deferring an idle timeout is appropriate
requirements.statement: Application protocols that use QUIC SHOULD provide guidance on when deferring an idle timeout is appropriate.

requirements.title: Allow deferring idle timeout
requirements.statement: An implementation of QUIC MAY provide applications with an option to defer an idle timeout.

requirements.title: These states SHOULD persist for at least three times the current PTO interval as defined in [QUIC-RECOVERY]
requirements.statement: These states SHOULD persist for at least three times the current PTO interval as defined in [QUIC-RECOVERY].

requirements.title: That have some alternative means to ensure that late-arriving packets do not induce a response, such as those that are able to close the UDP socket, MAY end these states earlier to allow for faster resource recovery
requirements.statement: Endpoints that have some alternative means to ensure that late-arriving packets do not induce a response, such as those that are able to close the UDP socket, MAY end these states earlier to allow for faster resource recovery.

requirements.title: Servers that retain an open socket for accepting new connections SHOULD NOT end the closing or draining state early
requirements.statement: Servers that retain an open socket for accepting new connections SHOULD NOT end the closing or draining state early.

requirements.title: Once its closing or draining state ends, an endpoint SHOULD discard all connection state
requirements.statement: Once its closing or draining state ends, an endpoint SHOULD discard all connection state.

requirements.title: Send a Stateless Reset in response to any further incoming packets belonging to this connection
requirements.statement: The endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to any further incoming packets belonging to this connection.

requirements.title: Send CONNECTION_CLOSE to terminate immediately
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST send a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame to terminate the connection immediately.

requirements.title: Close all streams on CONNECTION_CLOSE
requirements.statement: A CONNECTION_CLOSE frame MUST cause all streams to immediately become closed.

requirements.title: Reset open streams implicitly
requirements.statement: Open streams MUST be implicitly reset when a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame is sent.
notes: Split from one sentence that also states that all streams become closed.

requirements.title: Enter closing state after sending close
requirements.statement: After sending a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame, an endpoint MUST immediately enter the closing state.

requirements.title: Enter draining state after receiving close
requirements.statement: After receiving a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame, endpoints MUST enter the draining state.

requirements.title: Close immediately on protocol violation
requirements.statement: Violations of the protocol MUST lead to an immediate close.

requirements.title: Permit immediate close after application shutdown
requirements.statement: An immediate close MAY be used after an application protocol has arranged to close a connection.

requirements.title: Limit the rate at which it generates packets in the closing state
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD limit the rate at which it generates packets in the closing state.

requirements.title: An endpoint's selected connection ID and the QUIC version are sufficient information to identify packets for a closing connection
requirements.statement: An endpoint's selected connection ID and the QUIC version are sufficient information to identify packets for a closing connection; the endpoint MAY discard all other connection state.

requirements.title: Retain packet protection keys for incoming packets to allow it to read and process a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY retain packet protection keys for incoming packets to allow it to read and process a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame.

requirements.title: Drop packet protection keys when entering the closing state and send a packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in response to any UDP datagram that is received
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY drop packet protection keys when entering the closing state and send a packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in response to any UDP datagram that is received.

requirements.title: To avoid being used for an amplification attack, such endpoints MUST limit the cumulative size of packets it sends to three times the cumulative size of the packets that are received and attributed to the connection
requirements.statement: To avoid being used for an amplification attack, such endpoints MUST limit the cumulative size of packets it sends to three times the cumulative size of the packets that are received and attributed to the connection.

requirements.title: To minimize the state that an endpoint maintains for a closing connection, endpoints MAY send the exact same packet in response to any received packet
requirements.statement: To minimize the state that an endpoint maintains for a closing connection, endpoints MAY send the exact same packet in response to any received packet.

requirements.title: In the closing state MUST either discard packets received from an unvalidated address or limit the cumulative size of packets it sends to an unvalidated address to three times the size of packets it receives from that address
requirements.statement: An endpoint in the closing state MUST either discard packets received from an unvalidated address or limit the cumulative size of packets it sends to an unvalidated address to three times the size of packets it receives from that address.

requirements.title: Enter closing on immediate close
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST enter the closing state after initiating an immediate close.

requirements.title: Retain only closing-state essentials
requirements.statement: In the closing state, an endpoint MUST retain only enough information to generate a packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame and to identify packets as belonging to the connection.

requirements.title: Answer attributed packets with CONNECTION_CLOSE
requirements.statement: An endpoint in the closing state MUST send a packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in response to any incoming packet that it attributes to the connection.

requirements.title: While otherwise identical to the closing state, an endpoint in the draining state MUST NOT send any packets
requirements.statement: While otherwise identical to the closing state, an endpoint in the draining state MUST NOT send any packets.

requirements.title: That receives a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame MAY send a single packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame before entering the draining state, using a NO_ERROR code if appropriate
requirements.statement: An endpoint that receives a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame MAY send a single packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame before entering the draining state, using a NO_ERROR code if appropriate.

requirements.title: Send further packets
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT send further packets.

requirements.title: Enter the draining state from the closing state if it receives a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame, which indicates that the peer is also closing or draining
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY enter the draining state from the closing state if it receives a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame, which indicates that the peer is also closing or draining.

requirements.title: Enter draining on peer close
requirements.statement: The draining state MUST be entered once an endpoint receives a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame, which indicates that its peer is closing or draining.

requirements.title: After the handshake is confirmed (see Section 4
requirements.statement: After the handshake is confirmed (see Section 4.1.2 of [QUIC-TLS]), an endpoint MUST send any CONNECTION_CLOSE frames in a 1-RTT packet.

requirements.title: However, prior to confirming the handshake, it is possible that more advanced packet protection keys are not available to the peer, so another CONNECTION_CLOSE frame MAY be sent in a packet that uses a lower packet protection level
requirements.statement: However, prior to confirming the handshake, it is possible that more advanced packet protection keys are not available to the peer, so another CONNECTION_CLOSE frame MAY be sent in a packet that uses a lower packet protection level.

requirements.title: Under these circumstances, a server SHOULD send a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in both Handshake and Initial packets to ensure that at least one of them is processable by the client
requirements.statement: Under these circumstances, a server SHOULD send a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in both Handshake and Initial packets to ensure that at least one of them is processable by the client.

requirements.title: Prior to confirming the handshake, a peer might be unable to process 1-RTT packets, so an endpoint SHOULD send a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in both Handshake and 1-RTT packets
requirements.statement: Prior to confirming the handshake, a peer might be unable to process 1-RTT packets, so an endpoint SHOULD send a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in both Handshake and 1-RTT packets.

requirements.title: Also send a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in an Initial packet
requirements.statement: A server SHOULD also send a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in an Initial packet.

requirements.title: A CONNECTION_CLOSE of type 0x1d MUST be replaced by a CONNECTION_CLOSE of type 0x1c when sending the frame in Initial or Handshake packets
requirements.statement: A CONNECTION_CLOSE of type 0x1d MUST be replaced by a CONNECTION_CLOSE of type 0x1c when sending the frame in Initial or Handshake packets.

requirements.title: For this reason, endpoints MAY discard packets rather than immediately close if errors are detected in packets that lack authentication
requirements.statement: For this reason, endpoints MAY discard packets rather than immediately close if errors are detected in packets that lack authentication.

requirements.title: Send post-handshake close in 1-RTT
requirements.statement: After the handshake is confirmed, an endpoint MUST send any CONNECTION_CLOSE frames in a 1-RTT packet.

requirements.title: Allow lower-protection close before handshake confirmation
requirements.statement: Prior to confirming the handshake, another CONNECTION_CLOSE frame MAY be sent in a packet that uses a lower packet protection level.

requirements.title: Close in Handshake and 1-RTT before confirmation
requirements.statement: Prior to confirming the handshake, an endpoint SHOULD send a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame in both Handshake and 1-RTT packets.

requirements.title: Downgrade close type for Initial and Handshake
requirements.statement: A CONNECTION_CLOSE frame of type 0x1d MUST be replaced by a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame of type 0x1c when sending the frame in Initial or Handshake packets.

requirements.title: Clear Reason Phrase when downgrading close type
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST clear the value of the Reason Phrase field when converting to a CONNECTION_CLOSE of type 0x1c.
notes: Split from one sentence that also recommends APPLICATION_ERROR.

requirements.title: Prefer APPLICATION_ERROR when downgrading close type
requirements.statement: Endpoints SHOULD use the APPLICATION_ERROR code when converting to a CONNECTION_CLOSE of type 0x1c.
notes: Split from one sentence that also requires clearing the Reason Phrase field.

requirements.title: Send a Stateless Reset in response to receiving a packet that it cannot associate with an active connection
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to receiving a packet that it cannot associate with an active connection.

requirements.title: That wishes to communicate a fatal connection error MUST use a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame if it is able
requirements.statement: An endpoint that wishes to communicate a fatal connection error MUST use a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame if it is able.

requirements.title: Use a hard-to-guess 16-byte stateless reset token
requirements.statement: A stateless reset token MUST be 16 bytes long and difficult to guess.

requirements.title: Bind stateless reset tokens to connection IDs
requirements.statement: A stateless reset token MUST be specific to a connection ID.

requirements.title: Use the Figure 10 packet layout for Stateless Reset
requirements.statement: An endpoint that receives packets that it cannot process MUST send a packet in the layout shown in Figure 10.
notes: The figure body items define the reset packet layout and are captured separately in the review notes as packet-structure evidence.

requirements.title: Send Stateless Reset as a short-header packet
requirements.statement: A Stateless Reset MUST use an entire UDP datagram, starting with the first two bits of the packet header.

requirements.title: The remainder of the first byte and an arbitrary number of bytes following it are set to values that SHOULD be indistinguishable from random
requirements.statement: The remainder of the first byte and an arbitrary number of bytes following it are set to values that SHOULD be indistinguishable from random.

requirements.title: Include at least 38 unpredictable bits
requirements.statement: For the Stateless Reset to appear as a valid QUIC packet, the Unpredictable Bits field MUST include at least 38 bits of data.

requirements.title: Pad packets to keep Stateless Reset hard to distinguish
requirements.statement: To achieve that end, the endpoint SHOULD ensure that all packets it sends are at least 22 bytes longer than the minimum connection ID length that it requests the peer to include in its packets, adding PADDING frames as necessary.

requirements.title: Make short-packet Stateless Resets one byte shorter
requirements.statement: An endpoint that sends a Stateless Reset in response to a packet that is 43 bytes or shorter SHOULD send a Stateless Reset that is one byte shorter than the packet it responds to.

requirements.title: Limit Stateless Reset amplification
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT send a Stateless Reset that is three times or more larger than the packet it receives.

requirements.title: Discard too-small packets
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST discard packets that are too small to be valid QUIC packets.

requirements.title: Format Stateless Resets with a short header
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST send Stateless Resets formatted as a packet with a short header.

requirements.title: Treat any packet ending in a valid reset token as a Stateless Reset
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST treat any packet ending in a valid stateless reset token as a Stateless Reset.

requirements.title: Allow Stateless Reset in response to long-header packets
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to a packet with a long header.

requirements.title: Make reset tokens hard to guess
requirements.statement: The stateless reset token MUST be difficult to guess.

requirements.title: Advertise reset tokens in NEW_CONNECTION_ID
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST issue a stateless reset token by including the value in the Stateless Reset Token field of a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame.

requirements.title: Permit server transport-parameter reset tokens
requirements.statement: Servers MAY issue a stateless_reset_token transport parameter during the handshake that applies to the connection ID that it selected during the handshake.

requirements.title: End the connection on received reset
requirements.statement: If a peer receives a Stateless Reset, it MUST immediately end the connection.

requirements.title: Invalidate reset tokens when IDs retire
requirements.statement: Tokens MUST be invalidated when their associated connection ID is retired via a RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frame.

requirements.title: Send reset using the Figure 10 layout
requirements.statement: An endpoint that receives packets it cannot process MUST send a packet using the Stateless Reset layout shown in Figure 10.

requirements.title: Set fixed bits to 1
requirements.statement: Stateless Reset packets MUST set the fixed bits to 1.

requirements.title: Make leading reset bytes look random
requirements.statement: The remainder of the first byte and an arbitrary number of bytes following it SHOULD be indistinguishable from random.

requirements.title: Place the token in the tail
requirements.statement: The last 16 bytes of a Stateless Reset datagram MUST contain a stateless reset token.

requirements.title: Provide enough unpredictable bits
requirements.statement: The Unpredictable Bits field MUST include at least 38 bits of data.

requirements.title: Use a full UDP datagram
requirements.statement: A Stateless Reset MUST use an entire UDP datagram starting with the first two bits of the packet header.

requirements.title: Pad packets to improve reset indistinguishability
requirements.statement: To achieve that end, the endpoint SHOULD ensure that all packets it sends are at least 22 bytes longer than the minimum connection ID length it requests the peer to include in its packets, adding PADDING frames as necessary.

requirements.title: Avoid amplification with Stateless Reset
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT send a Stateless Reset that is three times or more larger than the packet it receives to avoid being used for amplification.

requirements.title: Support all negotiated versions in reset generation
requirements.statement: An endpoint that supports multiple versions of QUIC MUST generate a Stateless Reset that will be accepted by peers that support any version that the endpoint might support or might have supported prior to losing state.
notes: A faithful normative restatement here is version-specific and easy to overstate.

requirements.title: Use the trailing 16 bytes to detect Stateless Reset
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST use the trailing 16 bytes of the UDP datagram to detect a potential Stateless Reset.

requirements.title: Remember recent stateless reset tokens
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST remember all stateless reset tokens associated with the connection IDs and remote addresses for datagrams it has recently sent.

requirements.title: Compare the remote-address token set to classify Stateless Reset
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST identify a received datagram as a Stateless Reset by comparing the last 16 bytes of the datagram with all stateless reset tokens associated with the remote address on which the datagram was received.

requirements.title: Skip the token check after successful packet processing
requirements.statement: Endpoints MAY skip this check if any packet from a datagram is successfully processed.

requirements.title: Always check when the first packet cannot be processed
requirements.statement: However, the comparison MUST be performed when the first packet in an incoming datagram either cannot be associated with a connection or cannot be decrypted.

requirements.title: Ignore unused and retired reset tokens
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT check for any stateless reset tokens associated with connection IDs it has not used or for connection IDs that have been retired.

requirements.title: Avoid leaking token values during comparison
requirements.statement: When comparing a datagram to stateless reset token values, endpoints MUST perform the comparison without leaking information about the value of the token.

requirements.title: Enter draining on a matching stateless reset token
requirements.statement: If the last 16 bytes of the datagram are identical in value to a stateless reset token, the endpoint MUST enter the draining period and not send any further packets on this connection.

requirements.title: Detect potential reset with the last 16 bytes
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST detect a potential Stateless Reset using the trailing 16 bytes of the UDP datagram.

requirements.title: Remember and scope reset tokens
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST remember all stateless reset tokens associated with connection IDs and remote addresses for datagrams it has recently sent, including Stateless Reset Token field values from NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames and the server's transport parameters, and excluding tokens associated with connection IDs that are unused or retired.
notes: This sentence pair is treated as one atomic memory-scope requirement.

requirements.title: Enter draining on token match
requirements.statement: If the last 16 bytes of the datagram are identical in value to a stateless reset token, the endpoint MUST enter the draining period.

requirements.title: Stop sending after token match
requirements.statement: If the last 16 bytes of the datagram are identical in value to a stateless reset token, the endpoint MUST NOT send any further packets on this connection.

requirements.title: Be difficult to guess
requirements.statement: The stateless reset token MUST be difficult to guess.

requirements.title: Truncate the stateless reset token to 16 bytes
requirements.statement: The output of this function MUST be truncated to 16 bytes to produce the stateless reset token for that connection.

requirements.title: Use a recoverable connection ID length with static-key tokens
requirements.statement: An endpoint that uses this design MUST either use the same connection ID length for all connections or encode the length of the connection ID such that it can be recovered without state.

requirements.title: Disallow zero-length connection IDs for static-key tokens
requirements.statement: A static-key token function MUST NOT provide a zero-length connection ID.
notes: The RFC presents this as an additional design constraint rather than a standalone BCP14 sentence.

requirements.title: Do not reuse the connection ID and static key combination
requirements.statement: This method for choosing the stateless reset token means that the combination of connection ID and static key MUST NOT be used for another connection.

requirements.title: Do not reuse resetted connection IDs across shared static keys
requirements.statement: A connection ID from a connection that is reset by revealing the stateless reset token MUST NOT be reused for new connections at nodes that share a static key.

requirements.title: Use each stateless reset token for only one connection ID
requirements.statement: The same stateless reset token MUST NOT be used for multiple connection IDs.

requirements.title: Allow duplicate stateless reset tokens to signal protocol violation
requirements.statement: Endpoints are not required to compare new values against all previous values, but a duplicate value MAY be treated as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

requirements.title: Allow per-connection random secrets
requirements.statement: In order to create a stateless reset token, an endpoint MAY randomly generate a secret for every connection that it creates.

requirements.title: Allow static-key token generation
requirements.statement: A single static key MAY be used across all connections to the same endpoint by generating the proof using a pseudorandom function that takes a static key and the connection ID chosen by the endpoint as input.

requirements.title: Reuse the same generation method after state loss
requirements.statement: An endpoint that loses state MAY use the same method to generate a valid stateless reset token.

requirements.title: Forbid zero-length connection IDs
requirements.statement: An endpoint that uses this design MUST NOT provide a zero-length connection ID.

requirements.title: Keep Stateless Reset smaller than the triggering packet
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST ensure that every Stateless Reset that it sends is smaller than the packet that triggered it, unless it maintains state sufficient to prevent looping.

requirements.title: Allow a reset-send limit
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY remember the number of Stateless Resets that it has sent and stop generating new Stateless Resets once a limit is reached.

requirements.title: that detects an error SHOULD signal the existence of that error to its peer
requirements.statement: An endpoint that detects an error SHOULD signal the existence of that error to its peer.

requirements.title: The most appropriate error code (Section 20) SHOULD be included in the frame that signals the error
requirements.statement: The most appropriate error code (Section 20) SHOULD be included in the frame that signals the error.

requirements.title: Use a generic error code in place of a specific error code
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY use a generic error code such as PROTOCOL_VIOLATION or INTERNAL_ERROR in place of a specific error code.

requirements.title: Use any applicable error code
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY use any applicable error code when it detects an error condition.

requirements.title: A stateless reset MUST NOT be used by an endpoint that has the state necessary to send a frame on the connection
requirements.statement: A stateless reset MUST NOT be used by an endpoint that has the state necessary to send a frame on the connection.

requirements.title: Errors that result in the connection being unusable, such as an obvious violation of protocol semantics or corruption of state that affects an entire connection, MUST be signaled using a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame (Section 19
requirements.statement: Errors that result in the connection being unusable, such as an obvious violation of protocol semantics or corruption of state that affects an entire connection, MUST be signaled using a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame (Section 19.19).

requirements.title: Use CONNECTION_CLOSE type 0x1d for application-specific protocol errors
requirements.statement: Application-specific protocol errors MUST be signaled using the CONNECTION_CLOSE frame with a frame type of 0x1d.

requirements.title: Use CONNECTION_CLOSE type 0x1c for transport errors
requirements.statement: Transport errors, including all those described in this document, MUST be carried in the CONNECTION_CLOSE frame with a frame type of 0x1c.

requirements.title: be prepared to retransmit a packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame if it receives more packets on a terminated connection
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD be prepared to retransmit a packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame if it receives more packets on a terminated connection.

requirements.title: Limiting the number of retransmissions and the time over which this final packet is sent limits the effort expended on terminated connections
requirements.statement: Limiting the number of retransmissions and the time over which this final packet MUST be sent limits the effort expended on terminated connections.

requirements.title: An endpoint that continues to receive data for a terminated connection MUST attempt the stateless reset process
requirements.statement: An endpoint that continues to receive data for a terminated connection MUST attempt the stateless reset process (Section 10.3).

requirements.title: As the AEAD for Initial packets does not provide strong authentication, an endpoint MAY discard an invalid Initial packet
requirements.statement: As the AEAD for Initial packets does not provide strong authentication, an endpoint MAY discard an invalid Initial packet.

requirements.title: Only discard a packet without processing or after reverting effects
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT discard a packet unless it does not process the frames in the packet or it reverts the effects of any processing.

requirements.title: If an application-level error affects a single stream but otherwise leaves the connection in a recoverable state, the endpoint can send a RESET_STREAM frame (Section 19
requirements.statement: If an application-level error MUST affects a single stream but otherwise leaves the connection in a recoverable state, the endpoint can send a RESET_STREAM frame (Section 19.4) with an appropriate error code to terminate just the affected stream.

requirements.title: RESET_STREAM MUST only be instigated by the application protocol that uses QUIC
requirements.statement: RESET_STREAM MUST only be instigated by the application protocol that uses QUIC.

requirements.title: Allow stream termination only by the application protocol
requirements.statement: A stream MUST only be terminated by the application protocol.

requirements.title: A local instance of the application protocol uses a direct API call, and a remote instance uses the STOP_SENDING frame, which triggers an automatic RESET_STREAM
requirements.statement: A local instance of MUST the application protocol uses a direct API call, and a remote instance uses the STOP_SENDING frame, which triggers an automatic RESET_STREAM.

requirements.title: Application protocols SHOULD define rules for handling streams that are prematurely canceled by either endpoint
requirements.statement: Application protocols SHOULD define rules for handling streams that are prematurely canceled by either endpoint.

requirements.title: Version Negotiation packets have no cryptographic protection
requirements.statement: Version Negotiation packets MUST NOT have cryptographic protection; see [QUIC-INVARIANTS].

requirements.title: Retry packets use an AEAD function [AEAD] to protect against accidental modification
requirements.statement: Retry packets use an MUST AEAD function [AEAD] to protect against accidental modification.

requirements.title: Initial packets use an AEAD function, the keys for which are derived using a value that is visible on the wire
requirements.statement: Initial packets use an MUST AEAD function, the keys for which are derived using a value that is visible on the wire.

requirements.title: Initial protection exists to ensure that the sender of the packet is on the network path
requirements.statement: Initial protection exists to MUST ensure that the sender of the packet is on the network path.

requirements.title: Any entity that receives an Initial packet from a client can recover the keys that will allow them to both read the contents of the packet and generate Initial packets that will be successfully authenticated at either endpoint
requirements.statement: Any entity that receives MUST an Initial packet from a client can recover the keys that will allow them to both read the contents of the packet and generate Initial packets that will be successfully authenticated at either endpoint.

requirements.title: The AEAD also protects Initial packets against accidental modification
requirements.statement: The AEAD also protects MUST Initial packets against accidental modification.

requirements.title: The cryptographic handshake ensures that only the communicating endpoints receive the corresponding keys for Handshake, 0-RTT, and 1-RTT packets
requirements.statement: The cryptographic handshake ensures MUST that only the communicating endpoints receive the corresponding keys for Handshake, 0-RTT, and 1-RTT packets.

requirements.title: Initial (Section 17
requirements.statement: Initial, 0-RTT, and Handshake packets MUST contain a Length field that determines the end of the packet.

requirements.title: The length includes both the Packet Number and Payload fields, both of which are confidentiality protected and initially of unknown length
requirements.statement: The length includes both MUST the Packet Number and Payload fields, both of which are confidentiality protected and initially of unknown length.

requirements.title: Using the Length field, a sender can coalesce multiple QUIC packets into one UDP datagram
requirements.statement: Using the Length MUST field, a sender can coalesce multiple QUIC packets into one UDP datagram.

requirements.title: Receivers MUST be able to process coalesced packets
requirements.statement: Receivers MUST be able to process coalesced packets.

requirements.title: Place short-header packets last in a UDP datagram
requirements.statement: A packet with a short header MUST be the last packet included in a UDP datagram.

requirements.title: include multiple frames in a single packet if they are to be sent at the same encryption level, instead of coalescing multiple packets at the same encryption level
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD include multiple frames in a single packet if they are to be sent at the same encryption level, instead of coalescing multiple packets at the same encryption level.

requirements.title: Receivers MAY route based on the information in the first packet contained in a UDP datagram
requirements.statement: Receivers MAY route based on the information in the first packet contained in a UDP datagram.

requirements.title: Senders MUST NOT coalesce QUIC packets with different connection IDs into a single UDP datagram
requirements.statement: Senders MUST NOT coalesce QUIC packets with different connection IDs into a single UDP datagram.

requirements.title: Receivers SHOULD ignore any subsequent packets with a different Destination Connection ID than the first packet in the datagram
requirements.statement: Receivers SHOULD ignore any subsequent packets with a different Destination Connection ID than the first packet in the datagram.

requirements.title: Buffer or discard on decryption failure
requirements.statement: If decryption fails, the receiver MAY either discard or buffer the packet for later processing.

requirements.title: Continue processing remaining coalesced packets
requirements.statement: If decryption fails, the receiver MUST attempt to process the remaining packets.

requirements.title: Process each coalesced packet separately
requirements.statement: The receiver of coalesced QUIC packets MUST individually process each QUIC packet and separately acknowledge each QUIC packet, as if the packets were received as the payload of different UDP datagrams.

requirements.title: Retry packets (Section 17
requirements.statement: Retry packets (Section 17.2.5), Version Negotiation packets (Section 17.2.1), and packets with a short header (Section 17.3) MUST NOT contain a Length field or be followed by other packets in the same UDP datagram.

requirements.title: This number is used in determining the cryptographic nonce for packet protection
requirements.statement: This number MUST be used in determining the cryptographic nonce for packet protection.

requirements.title: maintains a separate packet number for sending and receiving
requirements.statement: Each endpoint maintains a MUST separate packet number for sending and receiving.

requirements.title: Version Negotiation (Section 17
requirements.statement: Version Negotiation (Section 17.2.1) and Retry (Section 17.2.5) packets MUST NOT include a packet number.

requirements.title: Initial packets can only be sent with Initial packet protection keys and acknowledged in packets that are also Initial packets
requirements.statement: Initial packets MUST only be sent with Initial packet protection keys and acknowledged in packets that are also Initial packets.

requirements.title: Similarly, Handshake packets are sent at the Handshake encryption level and can only be acknowledged in Handshake packets
requirements.statement: Handshake packets MUST be sent at the Handshake encryption level and be acknowledged only in Handshake packets.

requirements.title: numbers in each space start at packet number 0
requirements.statement: Packet numbers in each MUST space start at packet number 0.

requirements.title: Subsequent packets sent in the same packet number space MUST increase the packet number by at least one
requirements.statement: Subsequent packets sent in the same packet number space MUST increase the packet number by at least one.

requirements.title: A QUIC endpoint MUST NOT reuse a packet number within the same packet number space in one connection
