requirements.statement: A QUIC endpoint MUST NOT reuse a packet number within the same packet number space in one connection.

requirements.title: Allow Stateless Reset after packet-number exhaustion
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY send a Stateless Reset in response to further packets that it receives after packet-number exhaustion.

requirements.title: Close on packet-number exhaustion
requirements.statement: If the packet number for sending reaches 2^62-1, the sender MUST close the connection without sending a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame or any further packets.

requirements.title: discard a newly unprotected packet unless it is certain that it has not processed another packet with the same packet number from the same packet number space
requirements.statement: A receiver MUST discard a newly unprotected packet unless it is certain that it has not processed another packet with the same packet number from the same packet number space.

requirements.title: Duplicate suppression MUST happen after removing packet protection for the reasons described in Section 9
requirements.statement: Duplicate suppression MUST happen after removing packet protection for the reasons described in Section 9.5 of [QUIC-TLS].

requirements.title: Version Negotiation, Stateless Reset, and Retry packets do not contain frames
requirements.statement: Version Negotiation, Stateless Reset, and Retry packets MUST NOT contain frames.

requirements.title: Allow multiple frames and frame types in a packet payload
requirements.statement: The payload of a packet that contains frames MAY contain multiple frames and multiple frame types.

requirements.title: Require at least one frame in packet payloads
requirements.statement: The payload of a packet that contains frames MUST contain at least one frame.

requirements.title: Treat empty packet payloads as protocol violations
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST treat receipt of a packet containing no frames as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

requirements.title: Keep frames within one packet
requirements.statement: Frames MUST fit within a single QUIC packet rather than span multiple packets.

requirements.title: Prefix each frame with a Frame Type
requirements.statement: Each frame MUST begin with a Frame Type, followed by additional type-dependent fields.

requirements.title: A description of this summary is included after the table
requirements.statement: A description of this summary MUST be included after the table.

requirements.title: Use Frame Type for frame-specific flags in selected frames
requirements.statement: The Frame Type in ACK, STREAM, MAX_STREAMS, STREAMS_BLOCKED, and CONNECTION_CLOSE frames MUST be used to carry other frame-specific flags.

requirements.title: Use Frame Type as the frame identifier for other frames
requirements.statement: For all other frames, the Frame Type field MUST simply identify the frame.

requirements.title: Limit Initial and Handshake appearance of CONNECTION_CLOSE type 0x1c
requirements.statement: Only a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame of type 0x1c MAY appear in Initial or Handshake packets.

requirements.title: Allow all frames in 1-RTT packets
requirements.statement: All frames MAY appear in 1-RTT packets.

requirements.title: treat receipt of a frame in a packet type that is not permitted as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST treat receipt of a frame in a packet type that is not permitted as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

requirements.title: Treat N-marked packets as not ack-eliciting
requirements.statement: Packets containing only frames with the N marking MUST NOT be ack-eliciting.

requirements.title: Exclude C-marked packets from bytes in flight
requirements.statement: Packets containing only frames with the C marking MUST NOT count toward bytes in flight for congestion control purposes.

requirements.title: Allow P-marked packets to probe new paths
requirements.statement: Packets containing only frames with the P marking MAY be used to probe new network paths during connection migration.

requirements.title: Treat F-marked frame contents as flow controlled
requirements.statement: The contents of frames with the F marking MUST be flow controlled.

requirements.title: Treat unknown frame types as frame encoding errors
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST treat receipt of a frame of unknown type as a connection error of type FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR.

requirements.title: Make frames idempotent
requirements.statement: All frames MUST be idempotent in this version of QUIC.

requirements.title: That is, a valid frame does not cause undesirable side effects or errors when received more than once
requirements.statement: That is, a valid frame MUST NOT cause undesirable side effects or errors when received more than once.

requirements.title: Use the shortest possible frame-type encoding
requirements.statement: A frame type MUST use the shortest possible encoding.

requirements.title: Allow longer-than-necessary frame-type encodings to fail
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY treat receipt of a frame type that uses a longer encoding than necessary as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

requirements.title: The rules here generalize those of TLS, in that frames associated with establishing the connection can usually appear in packets in any packet number space, whereas those associated with transferring data can only appear in the application data packet number space
requirements.statement: The rules here generalize those of TLS, in that frames associated with establishing the connection can usually appear in packets in any packet number space, whereas those associated with transferring data MUST only appear in the application data packet number space:

requirements.title: Allow PADDING, PING, and CRYPTO in any packet number space
requirements.statement: PADDING, PING, and CRYPTO frames MAY appear in any packet number space.

requirements.title: Allow QUIC-layer CONNECTION_CLOSE in any packet number space
requirements.statement: CONNECTION_CLOSE frames signaling errors at the QUIC layer MAY appear in any packet number space.

requirements.title: Restrict application-error CONNECTION_CLOSE to application data
requirements.statement: CONNECTION_CLOSE frames signaling application errors MUST only appear in the application data packet number space.

requirements.title: Allow ACK in any packet number space but only acknowledge within that space
requirements.statement: ACK frames MAY appear in any packet number space, but they can only acknowledge packets that appeared in that packet number space.

requirements.title: Restrict all other frame types to application data
requirements.statement: All other frame types MUST only be sent in the application data packet number space.

requirements.title: treat receipt of these frames in 0-RTT packets as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION
requirements.statement: A server MAY treat receipt of these frames in 0-RTT packets as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

requirements.title: Send one or more frames in each QUIC packet
requirements.statement: A sender MUST send one or more frames in a QUIC packet.

requirements.title: wait for a short period of time to collect multiple frames before sending a packet that is not maximally packed, to avoid sending out large numbers of small packets
requirements.statement: A sender MAY wait for a short period of time to collect multiple frames before sending a packet that is not maximally packed, to avoid sending out large numbers of small packets.

requirements.title: An implementation MAY use knowledge about application sending behavior or heuristics to determine whether and for how long to wait
requirements.statement: An implementation MAY use knowledge about application sending behavior or heuristics to determine whether and for how long to wait.

requirements.title: A single QUIC packet can include multiple STREAM frames from one or more streaMs
requirements.statement: A single QUIC packet MUST can include multiple STREAM frames from one or more streaMs.

requirements.title: Implementations are advised to include as few streams as necessary in outgoing packets without losing transmission efficiency to underfilled packets
requirements.statement: Implementations are advised to MUST include as few streams as necessary in outgoing packets without losing transmission efficiency to underfilled packets.

requirements.title: A packet MUST NOT be acknowledged until packet protection has been successfully removed and all frames contained in the packet have been processed
requirements.statement: A packet MUST NOT be acknowledged until packet protection has been successfully removed and all frames contained in the packet have been processed.

requirements.title: For STREAM frames, this means the data has been enqueued in preparation to be received by the application protocol, but it does not require that data be delivered and consumed
requirements.statement: For STREAM frames, this means the data has been enqueued in preparation to be received by the application protocol, but it MUST NOT require that data be delivered and consumed.

requirements.title: Acknowledge processed packets with ACK frames
requirements.statement: Once the packet has been fully processed, a receiver MUST acknowledge receipt by sending one or more ACK frames containing the packet number of the received packet.

requirements.title: treat receipt of an acknowledgment for a packet it did not send as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION, if it is able to detect the condition
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD treat receipt of an acknowledgment for a packet it did not send as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION, if it is able to detect the condition.

requirements.title: Acknowledge all received and processed packets
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST acknowledge all packets they receive and process.

requirements.title: Send ACK frames within max_ack_delay only for ack-eliciting packets
requirements.statement: Only ack-eliciting packets MUST cause an ACK frame to be sent within the maximum ack delay.

requirements.title: Acknowledge non-ack-eliciting packets only for other reasons
requirements.statement: Packets that are not ack-eliciting MUST only be acknowledged when an ACK frame is sent for other reasons.

requirements.title: When sending a packet for any reason, an endpoint SHOULD attempt to include an ACK frame if one has not been sent recently
requirements.statement: When sending a packet for any reason, an endpoint SHOULD attempt to include an ACK frame if one has not been sent recently.

requirements.title: Acknowledge ack-eliciting packets within max_ack_delay
requirements.statement: Ack-eliciting packets MUST be acknowledged at least once within the maximum delay the endpoint communicated using the max_ack_delay transport parameter.

requirements.title: Acknowledge every packet at least once
requirements.statement: Every packet SHOULD be acknowledged at least once.

requirements.title: uses the receiver's max_ack_delay value in determining timeouts for timer-based retransmission, as detailed in Section 6
requirements.statement: A sender uses the MUST receiver's max_ack_delay value in determining timeouts for timer-based retransmission, as detailed in Section 6.2 of [QUIC-RECOVERY].

requirements.title: Acknowledge 0-RTT and 1-RTT packets within max_ack_delay
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST acknowledge all ack-eliciting 0-RTT and 1-RTT packets within its advertised max_ack_delay.

requirements.title: Acknowledge Initial and Handshake packets immediately
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST acknowledge all ack-eliciting Initial and Handshake packets immediately.

requirements.title: Do not send multiple ACK-only packets in response to one ack-eliciting packet
requirements.statement: Since packets containing only ACK frames are not congestion controlled, an endpoint MUST NOT send more than one such packet in response to receiving an ack-eliciting packet.

requirements.title: Do not send non-ack-eliciting packets in response to non-ack-eliciting packets
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT send a non-ack-eliciting packet in response to a non-ack-eliciting packet, even if there are packet gaps that precede the received packet.

requirements.title: Non-ack-eliciting packets are eventually acknowledged when the endpoint sends an ACK frame in response to other events
requirements.statement: Non-ack-eliciting packets are eventually MUST acknowledged when the endpoint sends an ACK frame in response to other events.

requirements.title: that is only sending ACK frames will not receive acknowledgments from its peer unless those acknowledgments are included in packets with ack-eliciting frames
requirements.statement: An endpoint that is only sending ACK frames will not receive acknowledgments from its peer unless those acknowledgments MUST be included in packets with ack-eliciting frames.

requirements.title: Send ACK frames with other frames when there is new ack-eliciting data
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD send an ACK frame with other frames when there are new ack-eliciting packets to acknowledge.

requirements.title: Delay ACK frames for non-ack-eliciting packets until needed
requirements.statement: When only non-ack-eliciting packets need to be acknowledged, an endpoint MAY choose not to send an ACK frame with outgoing frames until an ack-eliciting packet has been received.

requirements.title: Avoid infinite feedback loops when adding ack-eliciting frames
requirements.statement: In that case, an endpoint MUST NOT send an ack-eliciting frame in all packets that would otherwise be non-ack-eliciting.

requirements.title: Send ACK frames without delay for out-of-order or gap-detecting packets
requirements.statement: To assist loss detection at the sender, an endpoint SHOULD generate and send an ACK frame without delay when it receives an ack-eliciting packet that has a packet number less than another ack-eliciting packet that has been received, or when the packet has a packet number larger than the highest-numbered ack-eliciting packet that has been received and there are missing packets between that packet and this packet.

requirements.title: ACK ECN-CE packets immediately
requirements.statement: Packets marked with the ECN Congestion Experienced (CE) codepoint in the IP header SHOULD be acknowledged immediately.

requirements.title: determines how frequently to send acknowledgments in response to ack-eliciting packets
requirements.statement: A receiver determines how MUST frequently to send acknowledgments in response to ack-eliciting packets.

requirements.title: Send ACK after at least two ack-eliciting packets
requirements.statement: A receiver SHOULD send an ACK frame after receiving at least two ack-eliciting packets.

requirements.title: Allow processing multiple packets before deciding to ACK
requirements.statement: A receiver MAY process multiple available packets before determining whether to send an ACK frame in response.

requirements.title: When an ACK frame is sent, one or more ranges of acknowledged packets are included
requirements.statement: When an ACK frame is sent, one or more ranges of acknowledged packets MUST be included.

requirements.title: Acknowledge the most recently received packets
requirements.statement: ACK frames SHOULD always acknowledge the most recently received packets.

requirements.title: If it does not, then older ranges (those with the smallest packet numbers) are omitted
requirements.statement: If it MUST NOT, then older ranges (those with the smallest packet numbers) are omitted.

requirements.title: Limit remembered ACK ranges
requirements.statement: A receiver MUST limit the number of ACK Ranges it remembers and sends in ACK frames.

requirements.title: Stop tracking acknowledged ACK ranges
requirements.statement: After receiving acknowledgments for an ACK frame, the receiver SHOULD stop tracking those acknowledged ACK Ranges.

requirements.title: Senders can expect acknowledgments for most packets, but QUIC does not guarantee receipt of an acknowledgment for every packet that the receiver processes
requirements.statement: Senders can expect acknowledgments for most packets, but QUIC MUST NOT guarantee receipt of an acknowledgment for every packet that the receiver processes.

requirements.title: Allow discarding ACK ranges to limit ACK frame size
requirements.statement: A receiver MAY discard unacknowledged ACK Ranges to limit ACK frame size.

requirements.title: Discard ACK ranges when a frame would be too large
requirements.statement: A receiver MUST discard unacknowledged ACK Ranges if an ACK frame would be too large to fit in a packet.

requirements.title: Limit ACK frame size further when useful
requirements.statement: Receivers MAY also limit ACK frame size further to preserve space for other frames or to limit the capacity that acknowledgments consume.

requirements.title: Retain ACK ranges unless future packets cannot arrive there
requirements.statement: A receiver MUST retain an ACK Range unless it can ensure that it will not subsequently accept packets with numbers in that range.

requirements.title: Retain the largest successfully processed packet number
requirements.statement: Receivers MAY discard all ACK Ranges if they retain the largest packet number that has been successfully processed.

requirements.title: Include the largest received packet number in every ACK frame
requirements.statement: A receiver SHOULD include an ACK Range containing the largest received packet number in every ACK frame.

requirements.title: Section 13
requirements.statement: Section MUST 13.2.4 describes an exemplary approach for determining what packets to acknowledge in each ACK frame.

requirements.title: In cases with ACK frame loss and reordering, this approach does not guarantee that every acknowledgment is seen by the sender before it is no longer included in the ACK frame
requirements.statement: In cases with ACK frame loss and reordering, this approach MUST NOT guarantee that every acknowledgment is seen by the sender before it is no longer included in the ACK frame.

requirements.title: Measure intentional acknowledgment delays
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST measure the delays intentionally introduced between the time the packet with the largest packet number is received and the time an acknowledgment is sent.

requirements.title: Encode acknowledgment delay in ACK Delay
requirements.statement: The endpoint MUST encode this acknowledgment delay in the ACK Delay field of an ACK frame.

requirements.title: Do not include uncontrolled delays in ACK Delay
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT include delays that it does not control when populating the ACK Delay field in an ACK frame.

requirements.title: Include buffering delays from missing decryption keys
requirements.statement: Endpoints SHOULD include buffering delays caused by unavailability of decryption keys.

requirements.title: Report measured delay when it exceeds max_ack_delay
requirements.statement: When the measured acknowledgment delay is larger than its max_ack_delay, an endpoint SHOULD report the measured delay.

requirements.title: Carry ACK frames only in the same packet number space
requirements.statement: ACK frames MUST only be carried in a packet that has the same packet number space as the packet being acknowledged.

requirements.title: Use 1-RTT for acknowledging 1-RTT packets
requirements.statement: Packets that are protected with 1-RTT keys MUST be acknowledged in packets that are also protected with 1-RTT keys.

requirements.title: Acknowledge client 0-RTT packets with 1-RTT packets
requirements.statement: Packets that a client sends with 0-RTT packet protection MUST be acknowledged by the server in packets protected by 1-RTT keys.

requirements.title: This can mean that the client is unable to use these acknowledgments if the server cryptographic handshake messages are delayed or lost
requirements.statement: This can mean that MUST the client is unable to use these acknowledgments if the server cryptographic handshake messages are delayed or lost.

requirements.title: Send non-PADDING frames periodically to elicit acknowledgments
requirements.statement: To avoid a deadlock, a sender SHOULD ensure that other frames are sent periodically in addition to PADDING frames to elicit acknowledgments from the receiver.

requirements.title: Do not retransmit lost packets whole
requirements.statement: QUIC packets that are determined to be lost MUST NOT be retransmitted whole.

requirements.title: Do not retransmit lost frames whole
requirements.statement: The same rule MUST apply to the frames that are contained within lost packets.

requirements.title: Send lost information again in new frames
requirements.statement: Instead, the information that might be carried in frames MUST be sent again in new frames as needed.

requirements.title: Use new frames and packets for lost information
requirements.statement: New frames and packets MUST be used to carry information that is determined to have been lost.

requirements.title: Stop resending information once it is acknowledged
requirements.statement: In general, information MUST be sent again when a packet containing that information is determined to be lost and cease being sent when a packet containing that information is acknowledged.

requirements.title: Retransmit CRYPTO data until acknowledged
requirements.statement: Data sent in CRYPTO frames MUST be retransmitted according to the rules in [QUIC-RECOVERY] until all data has been acknowledged.

requirements.title: Discard CRYPTO data when its packet number space is discarded
requirements.statement: Data in CRYPTO frames for Initial and Handshake packets MUST be discarded when the keys for the corresponding packet number space are discarded.

requirements.title: Retransmit STREAM data unless RESET_STREAM was sent
requirements.statement: Application data sent in STREAM frames MUST be retransmitted in new STREAM frames unless the endpoint has sent a RESET_STREAM for that stream.

requirements.title: Stop needing STREAM frames after RESET_STREAM
requirements.statement: Once an endpoint sends a RESET_STREAM frame, it MAY omit further STREAM frames.

requirements.title: Carry the most recent acknowledgments and delay in ACK frames
requirements.statement: ACK frames MUST carry the most recent set of acknowledgments and the acknowledgment delay from the largest acknowledged packet.

requirements.title: Retransmit RESET_STREAM until acknowledged or data is acknowledged
requirements.statement: Cancellation of stream transmission, as carried in a RESET_STREAM frame, MUST be sent until acknowledged or until all stream data is acknowledged by the peer.

requirements.title: The content of a RESET_STREAM frame MUST NOT change when it is sent again
requirements.statement: The content of a RESET_STREAM frame MUST NOT change when it is sent again.

requirements.title: Retransmit STOP_SENDING until the stream reaches a terminal state
requirements.statement: A request to cancel stream transmission, as encoded in a STOP_SENDING frame, MUST be sent until the receiving part of the stream enters either a Data Recvd state or a Reset Recvd state.

requirements.title: Do not resend connection close signals
requirements.statement: Connection close signals, including packets that contain CONNECTION_CLOSE frames, MUST NOT be sent again when packet loss is detected.

requirements.title: Send current connection maximum data in MAX_DATA frames
requirements.statement: The current connection maximum data MUST be sent in MAX_DATA frames.

requirements.title: Send updated MAX_DATA values when the frame is lost or the limit changes
requirements.statement: An updated value MUST be sent in a MAX_DATA frame if the packet containing the most recently sent MAX_DATA frame is declared lost or when the endpoint decides to update the limit.

requirements.title: Send current stream data offsets in MAX_STREAM_DATA frames
requirements.statement: The current maximum stream data offset MUST be sent in MAX_STREAM_DATA frames.

requirements.title: Send updated MAX_STREAM_DATA values when the frame is lost or the limit changes
requirements.statement: Like MAX_DATA, an updated value MUST be sent when the packet containing the most recent MAX_STREAM_DATA frame for a stream is lost or when the limit is updated.

requirements.title: Stop sending MAX_STREAM_DATA once the stream is closed or reset
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD stop sending MAX_STREAM_DATA frames when the receiving part of the stream enters a Size Known or Reset Recvd state.

requirements.title: Send the stream-type limit in MAX_STREAMS frames
requirements.statement: The limit on streams of a given type MUST be sent in MAX_STREAMS frames.

requirements.title: Send updated MAX_STREAMS values when the frame is lost or the limit changes
requirements.statement: Like MAX_DATA, an updated value MUST be sent when a packet containing the most recent MAX_STREAMS for a stream type frame is declared lost or when the limit is updated.

requirements.title: Carry blocked signals in dedicated frames
requirements.statement: Blocked signals MUST be carried in DATA_BLOCKED, STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED, and STREAMS_BLOCKED frames.

requirements.title: Scope blocked signals appropriately
requirements.statement: DATA_BLOCKED, STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED, and STREAMS_BLOCKED frames MUST use connection, stream, and stream-type scope respectively.

requirements.title: Send a new blocked frame while blocked and the latest frame is lost
requirements.statement: A new frame MUST be sent if a packet containing the most recent frame for a scope is lost, but only while the endpoint is blocked on the corresponding limit.

requirements.title: Include the blocking limit in blocked frames
requirements.statement: These frames MUST always include the limit that is causing blocking at the time that they are transmitted.

requirements.title: Send PATH_CHALLENGE periodically until validation completes or is no longer needed
requirements.statement: A liveness or path validation check using PATH_CHALLENGE frames MUST be sent periodically until a matching PATH_RESPONSE frame is received or until there is no remaining need for liveness or path validation checking.

requirements.title: Use different PATH_CHALLENGE payloads each time
requirements.statement: PATH_CHALLENGE frames MUST include a different payload each time they are sent.

requirements.title: Send PATH_RESPONSE once
requirements.statement: Responses to path validation using PATH_RESPONSE frames MUST be sent just once.

requirements.title: Send new connection IDs in NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames and retransmit them if lost
requirements.statement: New connection IDs MUST be sent in NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames and retransmitted if the packet containing them is lost.

requirements.title: Send retired connection IDs in RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames and retransmit them if lost
requirements.statement: Retired connection IDs MUST be sent in RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames and retransmitted if the packet containing them is lost.

requirements.title: Compare NEW_TOKEN frames directly for duplicates and reordering
requirements.statement: No special support MUST be used for detecting reordered and duplicated NEW_TOKEN frames other than a direct comparison of the frame contents.

requirements.title: PING and PADDING frames contain no information, so lost PING or PADDING frames do not require repair
requirements.statement: PING and PADDING frames contain no information, so lost PING or PADDING frames MUST NOT require repair.

requirements.title: The HANDSHAKE_DONE frame MUST be retransmitted until it is acknowledged
requirements.statement: The HANDSHAKE_DONE frame MUST be retransmitted until it is acknowledged.

requirements.title: prioritize retransmission of data over sending new data, unless priorities specified by the application indicate otherwise
requirements.statement: Endpoints SHOULD prioritize retransmission of data over sending new data, unless priorities specified by the application indicate otherwise; see Section 2.3.

requirements.title: accept packets containing an outdated frame, such as a MAX_DATA frame carrying a smaller maximum data value than one found in an older packet
requirements.statement: A receiver MUST accept packets containing an outdated frame, such as a MAX_DATA frame carrying a smaller maximum data value than one found in an older packet.

requirements.title: avoid retransmitting information from packets once they are acknowledged
requirements.statement: A sender SHOULD avoid retransmitting information from packets once they are acknowledged.

requirements.title: This includes packets that are acknowledged after being declared lost, which can happen in the presence of network reordering
requirements.statement: This includes packets that MUST are acknowledged after being declared lost, which can happen in the presence of network reordering.

requirements.title: can discard this information after a period of time elapses that adequately allows for reordering, such as a PTO (Section 6
requirements.statement: A sender can discard MUST this information after a period of time elapses that adequately allows for reordering, such as a PTO (Section 6.2 of [QUIC-RECOVERY]), or based on other events, such as reaching a memory limit.

requirements.title: Take congestion control action when loss is detected
requirements.statement: Upon detecting losses, a sender MUST take appropriate congestion control action.

requirements.title: QUIC endpoints MAY use ECN to detect and respond to network congestion
requirements.statement: QUIC endpoints MAY use ECN [RFC3168] to detect and respond to network congestion.

requirements.title: Check whether the path and peer support ECN before enabling it
requirements.statement: To enable ECN, a sending QUIC endpoint MUST first determine whether a path supports ECN marking and whether the peer reports the ECN values in received IP headers.

requirements.title: Do not report ECN counts without ECN support or ECN field access
requirements.statement: If an endpoint does not implement ECN support or does not have access to received ECN fields, it MUST NOT report ECN counts for packets it receives.

requirements.title: Even if an endpoint does not set an ECT field in packets it sends, the endpoint MUST provide feedback about ECN markings it receives, if these are accessible
requirements.statement: Even if an endpoint does not set an ECT field in packets it sends, the endpoint MUST provide feedback about ECN markings it receives, if these are accessible.

requirements.title: Failing to report the ECN counts will cause the sender to disable the use of ECN for this connection
requirements.statement: Failing to report the MUST ECN counts will cause the sender to disable the use of ECN for this connection.

requirements.title: Update ECN counts when receiving marked IP packets
requirements.statement: On receiving an IP packet with an ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE codepoint, an ECN-enabled endpoint MUST access the ECN field and increase the corresponding ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE count.

requirements.title: Include ECN counts in subsequent ACK frames
requirements.statement: These ECN counts MUST be included in subsequent ACK frames.

requirements.title: Keep separate ECN counts per packet number space
requirements.statement: Each packet number space MUST maintain separate acknowledgment state and separate ECN counts.

requirements.title: Count ECN once per coalesced QUIC packet
requirements.statement: Coalesced QUIC packets share the same IP header so the ECN counts MUST be incremented once for each coalesced QUIC packet.

requirements.title: Count ECN for each packet number space in a coalesced datagram
requirements.statement: If one Initial, one Handshake, and one 1-RTT QUIC packet are coalesced into a single UDP datagram, the ECN counts for all three packet number spaces MUST be incremented by one each based on the ECN field of the single IP header.

requirements.title: Increment ECN counts only when received packets are processed
requirements.statement: ECN counts MUST only be incremented when QUIC packets from the received IP packet are processed.

requirements.title: Validate ECN counts per path and disable ECN on errors
requirements.statement: To ensure connectivity in the presence of such devices, an endpoint MUST validate the ECN counts for each network path and disable the use of ECN on that path if errors are detected.

requirements.title: Mark early packets on a new path with ECT(0)
requirements.statement: The endpoint MUST set an ECT(0) codepoint in the IP header of early outgoing packets sent on a new path to the peer.

requirements.title: Watch for ECN-marked packets being lost
requirements.statement: The endpoint MUST monitor whether all packets sent with an ECT codepoint are eventually deemed lost, indicating that ECN validation has failed.

requirements.title: Validate ECN for each new connection and path change
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST attempt to use ECN and validate this for each new connection, when switching to a server's preferred address, and on active connection migration to a new path.

requirements.title: Implementations MAY use other methods defined in RFCs
requirements.statement: Implementations MAY use other methods defined in RFCs; see [RFC8311].

requirements.title: Validate ECN using ECT(1) counts when ECT(1) is used
requirements.statement: Implementations that use the ECT(1) codepoint MUST perform ECN validation using the reported ECT(1) counts.

requirements.title: Validate ECN counts before using them
requirements.statement: An endpoint that receives an ACK frame with ECN counts MUST validate the counts before using them.

requirements.title: Fail ECN validation when counts are missing for newly acknowledged ECT packets
requirements.statement: If an ACK frame newly acknowledges a packet that the endpoint sent with either the ECT(0) or ECT(1) codepoint set, ECN validation MUST fail if the corresponding ECN counts are not present in the ACK frame.

requirements.title: This check detects a network element that zeroes the ECN field or a peer that does not report ECN markings
requirements.statement: This check detects a network element that zeroes the ECN field or a peer that MUST NOT report ECN markings.

requirements.title: Fail ECN validation when ECT(0) and CE counts are too small
requirements.statement: ECN validation MUST fail if the sum of the increase in ECT(0) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets that were originally sent with an ECT(0) marking.

requirements.title: Fail ECN validation when ECT(1) and CE counts are too small
requirements.statement: ECN validation MUST fail if the sum of the increases to ECT(1) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets sent with an ECT(1) marking.

requirements.title: Do not fail ECN validation for reordered ACK frames that do not increase the largest acknowledged packet number
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT fail ECN validation as a result of processing an ACK frame that does not increase the largest acknowledged packet number.

requirements.title: Fail ECN validation when received counts exceed sent counts
requirements.statement: ECN validation MUST fail if the received total count for either ECT(0) or ECT(1) exceeds the total number of packets sent with each corresponding ECT codepoint.

requirements.title: Fail ECN validation for never-applied ECT counts
requirements.statement: Validation MUST fail when an endpoint receives a non-zero ECN count corresponding to an ECT codepoint that it never applied.

requirements.title: Disable ECN when validation fails
requirements.statement: If validation fails, then the endpoint MUST disable ECN.

requirements.title: It stops setting the ECT codepoint in IP packets that it sends, assuming that either the network path or the peer does not support ECN
requirements.statement: It stops setting the ECT codepoint in IP packets that it sends, assuming that either the network path or the peer MUST NOT support ECN.

requirements.title: Even if validation fails, an endpoint MAY revalidate ECN for the same path at any later time in the connection
requirements.statement: Even if validation fails, an endpoint MAY revalidate ECN for the same path at any later time in the connection.

requirements.title: Upon successful validation, an endpoint MAY continue to set an ECT codepoint in subsequent packets it sends, with the expectation that the path is ECN capable
requirements.statement: Upon successful validation, an endpoint MAY continue to set an ECT codepoint in subsequent packets it sends, with the expectation that the path is ECN capable.

requirements.title: Network routing and path elements can change mid-connection
requirements.statement: Network routing and path elements can change mid-connection; an endpoint MUST disable ECN if validation later fails.

requirements.title: Allow one or more QUIC packets per UDP datagram
requirements.statement: A UDP datagram MAY include one or more QUIC packets.

requirements.title: The datagram size includes one or more QUIC packet headers and protected payloads, but not the UDP or IP headers
requirements.statement: The datagram size includes MUST one or more QUIC packet headers and protected payloads, but not the UDP or IP headers.

requirements.title: Define maximum datagram size as the largest UDP payload across a path
requirements.statement: The maximum datagram size MUST be defined as the largest size of UDP payload that can be sent across a network path using a single UDP datagram.

requirements.title: QUIC MUST NOT be used if the network path cannot support a maximum datagram size of at least 1200 bytes
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST NOT be used if the network path cannot support a maximum datagram size of at least 1200 bytes.

requirements.title: Use PMTUD or DPLPMTUD to discover larger datagram sizes
requirements.statement: Any maximum datagram size larger than 1200 bytes MUST be discovered using Path Maximum Transmission Unit Discovery (PMTUD) or Datagram Packetization Layer PMTU Discovery (DPLPMTUD).

requirements.title: Avoid exceeding the max_udp_payload_size once known
requirements.statement: A sender MUST avoid exceeding the max_udp_payload_size transport parameter limit once the value is known.

requirements.title: UDP datagrams MUST NOT be fragmented at the IP layer
requirements.statement: UDP datagrams MUST NOT be fragmented at the IP layer.

requirements.title: In IPv4 [IPv4], the Don't Fragment (DF) bit MUST be set if possible, to prevent fragmentation on the path
requirements.statement: In IPv4 [IPv4], the Don't Fragment (DF) bit MUST be set if possible, to prevent fragmentation on the path.

requirements.title: Treat received datagram size as unauthenticated
requirements.statement: If an endpoint receives a datagram of a certain size, it MUST NOT assume that the sender sent the datagram at the same size.

requirements.title: Pad Initial datagrams to 1200 bytes
requirements.statement: A client MUST expand the payload of all UDP datagrams carrying Initial packets to at least the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes by adding PADDING frames to the Initial packet or by coalescing the Initial packet.

requirements.title: Initial packets can even be coalesced with invalid packets, which a receiver will discard
requirements.statement: Initial packets can even MUST be coalesced with invalid packets, which a receiver will discard.

requirements.title: Pad server Initial datagrams to 1200 bytes
requirements.statement: A server MUST expand the payload of all UDP datagrams carrying ack-eliciting Initial packets to at least the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes.

requirements.title: Sending UDP datagrams of this size ensures that the network path supports a reasonable Path Maximum Transmission Unit (PMTU), in both directions
requirements.statement: Sending UDP datagrams of MUST this size ensures that the network path supports a reasonable Path Maximum Transmission Unit (PMTU), in both directions.

requirements.title: Allow oversized Initial datagrams when the path and peer support them
requirements.statement: Datagrams containing Initial packets MAY exceed 1200 bytes if the sender believes that the network path and peer both support the size that it chooses.

requirements.title: Discard undersized Initial packets at the server
requirements.statement: A server MUST discard an Initial packet that is carried in a UDP datagram with a payload that is smaller than the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes.

requirements.title: Allow servers to close on undersized Initial packets
requirements.statement: A server MAY also immediately close the connection by sending a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame with an error code of PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

requirements.title: Limit bytes sent before validating the client address
requirements.statement: The server MUST also limit the number of bytes it sends before validating the address of the client.

requirements.title: The UDP payload includes one or more QUIC packet headers and protected payloads
requirements.statement: The UDP payload includes MUST one or more QUIC packet headers and protected payloads.

requirements.title: The largest UDP payload an endpoint sends at any given time is referred to as the endpoint's maximum datagram size
requirements.statement: The largest UDP payload MUST an endpoint sends at any given time is referred to as the endpoint's maximum datagram size.

requirements.title: Use DPLPMTUD or PMTUD to determine support for a desired size
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD use DPLPMTUD or PMTUD to determine whether the path to a destination will support a desired maximum datagram size without fragmentation.

requirements.title: Do not exceed the smallest allowed maximum datagram size without PMTU discovery
requirements.statement: In the absence of these mechanisms, QUIC endpoints SHOULD NOT send datagrams larger than the smallest allowed maximum datagram size.

requirements.title: Both DPLPMTUD and PMTUD send datagrams that are larger than the current maximum datagram size, referred to as PMTU probes
requirements.statement: Both DPLPMTUD and PMTUD MUST send datagrams that are larger than the current maximum datagram size, referred to as PMTU probes.

requirements.title: Size non-PMTU-probe packets to fit within the maximum datagram size
requirements.statement: All QUIC packets that are not sent in a PMTU probe SHOULD be sized to fit within the maximum datagram size.

requirements.title: Stop sending packets on a path that cannot support 1200 bytes
requirements.statement: If a QUIC endpoint determines that the PMTU between any pair of local and remote IP addresses cannot support the smallest allowed maximum datagram size of 1200 bytes, it MUST immediately cease sending QUIC packets on the affected path except for those in PMTU probes or those containing CONNECTION_CLOSE frames.

requirements.title: Allow terminating the connection when no alternative path exists
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY terminate the connection if an alternative path cannot be found.

requirements.title: Maintain a maximum datagram size per address pair when doing PMTU discovery
requirements.statement: QUIC implementations that implement any kind of PMTU discovery SHOULD maintain a maximum datagram size for each combination of local and remote IP addresses.

requirements.title: Allow conservative maximum datagram size estimates
requirements.statement: A QUIC implementation MAY be more conservative in computing the maximum datagram size to allow for unknown tunnel overheads or IP header options or extensions.

requirements.title: Ignore ICMP messages that claim PMTU below the minimum
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST ignore an ICMP message that claims the PMTU has decreased below QUIC's smallest allowed maximum datagram size.

requirements.title: Validate ICMP messages when using PMTUD
requirements.statement: QUIC endpoints using PMTUD SHOULD validate ICMP messages to protect from packet injection as specified in [RFC8201] and Section 5.2 of [RFC8085].

requirements.title: Use the quoted packet to associate ICMP messages with a connection
requirements.statement: This validation SHOULD use the quoted packet supplied in the payload of an ICMP message to associate the message with a corresponding transport connection.

requirements.title: Validate ICMP messages with addresses, ports, and connection IDs
requirements.statement: ICMP message validation MUST include matching IP addresses and UDP ports and, when possible, connection IDs to an active QUIC session.

requirements.title: Ignore ICMP messages that fail validation
requirements.statement: The endpoint SHOULD ignore all ICMP messages that fail validation.

requirements.title: Do not increase PMTU based on ICMP messages
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST NOT increase the PMTU based on ICMP messages.

requirements.title: Allow provisional reductions in maximum datagram size
requirements.statement: Any reduction in QUIC's maximum datagram size in response to ICMP messages MAY be provisional until QUIC's loss detection algorithm determines that the quoted packet has actually been lost.

requirements.title: DPLPMTUD [DPLPMTUD] relies on tracking loss or acknowledgment of QUIC packets that are carried in PMTU probes
requirements.statement: DPLPMTUD [DPLPMTUD] relies on tracking loss or acknowledgment of QUIC packets that MUST be carried in PMTU probes.

requirements.title: PMTU probes for DPLPMTUD that use the PADDING frame implement "Probing using padding data", as defined in Section 4
requirements.statement: PMTU probes for DPLPMTUD MUST that use the PADDING frame implement "Probing using padding data", as defined in Section 4.1 of [DPLPMTUD].

requirements.title: Set BASE_PLPMTU consistently with QUIC's minimum
requirements.statement: Endpoints SHOULD set the initial value of BASE_PLPMTU to be consistent with QUIC's smallest allowed maximum datagram size.

requirements.title: Maintain an MPS per address pair when using DPLPMTUD
requirements.statement: QUIC endpoints implementing DPLPMTUD MUST maintain a DPLPMTUD Maximum Packet Size for each combination of local and remote IP addresses.

requirements.title: Make PMTU probes ack-eliciting
requirements.statement: PMTU probes MUST be ack-eliciting packets.

requirements.title: Do not treat PMTU probe loss as congestion
requirements.statement: Loss of a QUIC packet that is carried in a PMTU probe SHOULD NOT trigger a congestion control reaction.

requirements.title: Identify versions with 32-bit unsigned numbers
requirements.statement: QUIC versions MUST be identified using a 32-bit unsigned number.

requirements.title: Reserve version 0x00000000 for version negotiation
requirements.statement: The version 0x00000000 MUST be reserved to represent version negotiation.

requirements.title: Identify this specification as version 0x00000001
requirements.statement: This version of the specification MUST be identified by the number 0x00000001.

requirements.title: Use TLS for version 0x00000001
requirements.statement: Version 0x00000001 of QUIC MUST use TLS as a cryptographic handshake protocol.

requirements.title: Reserve versions with the top 16 bits cleared for future IETF consensus
requirements.statement: Versions with the most significant 16 bits of the version number cleared MUST be reserved for use in future IETF consensus documents.

requirements.title: Reserve 0x?a?a?a?a versions to force version negotiation
requirements.statement: Versions that follow the pattern 0x?a?a?a?a MUST be reserved for use in forcing version negotiation to be exercised.

requirements.title: or server MAY advertise support for any of these reserved versions
requirements.statement: A client or server MAY advertise support for any of these reserved versions.

requirements.title: Allow clients to use reserved versions to elicit version negotiation
requirements.statement: A client MAY use one of these version numbers with the expectation that the server will initiate version negotiation.

requirements.title: Allow servers to advertise reserved versions and expect clients to ignore them
requirements.statement: A server MAY advertise support for one of these versions and can expect that clients ignore the value.

requirements.title: Treat reserved versions as non-real protocols
requirements.statement: Reserved version numbers MUST never represent a real protocol.

requirements.title: The QUIC variable-length integer encoding MUST reserve the two most significant bits of the f...
requirements.statement: The QUIC variable-length integer encoding MUST reserve the two most significant bits of the first byte to encode the base-2 logarithm of the integer encoding length in bytes.

requirements.title: The integer value MUST be encoded on the remaining bits, in network byte order
requirements.statement: The integer value MUST be encoded on the remaining bits, in network byte order.

requirements.title: This means that integers are encoded on 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes and MAY encode 6-, 14-, 30-, or 6...
requirements.statement: This means that integers are encoded on 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes and MAY encode 6-, 14-, 30-, or 62-bit values, respectively.

requirements.title: Values MUST NOT need to be encoded on the minimum number of bytes necessary, with the sole ex...
requirements.statement: Values MUST NOT need to be encoded on the minimum number of bytes necessary, with the sole exception of the Frame Type field; see Section 12.4.

requirements.title: Versions (Section 15), packet numbers sent in the header (Section 17.1), and the length of co...
requirements.statement: Versions (Section 15), packet numbers sent in the header (Section 17.1), and the length of connection IDs in long header packets (Section 17.2) are described using integers but MUST NOT use this encoding.

requirements.title: All numeric values MUST be encoded in network byte order (that is, big endian), and all field...
requirements.statement: All numeric values MUST be encoded in network byte order (that is, big endian), and all field sizes are in bits.

requirements.title: When present in long or short packet headers, they MUST be encoded in 1 to 4 bytes
requirements.statement: When present in long or short packet headers, they MUST be encoded in 1 to 4 bytes.

requirements.title: Prior to receiving an acknowledgment for a packet number space, the full packet number MUST b...
requirements.statement: Prior to receiving an acknowledgment for a packet number space, the full packet number MUST be included; it is not to be truncated, as described below.

requirements.title: After an acknowledgment is received for a packet number space, the sender MUST use a packet n...
requirements.statement: After an acknowledgment is received for a packet number space, the sender MUST use a packet number size able to represent more than twice as large a range as the difference between the largest acknowledged packet number and the packet number being sent.

requirements.title: An endpoint SHOULD use a large enough packet number encoding to allow the packet number to be...
requirements.statement: An endpoint SHOULD use a large enough packet number encoding to allow the packet number to be recovered even if the packet arrives after packets that are sent afterwards.

requirements.title: The Header Form field MUST be 1 bits long with value 1
requirements.statement: The Header Form field MUST be 1 bits long with value 1.

requirements.title: The Fixed Bit field MUST be 1 bits long with value 1
requirements.statement: The Fixed Bit field MUST be 1 bits long with value 1.

requirements.title: The Long Packet Type field MUST be 2 bits long
requirements.statement: The Long Packet Type field MUST be 2 bits long.

requirements.title: The Type-Specific Bits field MUST be 4 bits long
requirements.statement: The Type-Specific Bits field MUST be 4 bits long.

requirements.title: The Version field MUST be 32 bits long
requirements.statement: The Version field MUST be 32 bits long.

requirements.title: The Destination Connection ID Length field MUST be 8 bits long
requirements.statement: The Destination Connection ID Length field MUST be 8 bits long.

requirements.title: The Destination Connection ID field MUST be between 0 and 160 bits long
requirements.statement: The Destination Connection ID field MUST be between 0 and 160 bits long.

requirements.title: The Source Connection ID Length field MUST be 8 bits long
requirements.statement: The Source Connection ID Length field MUST be 8 bits long.

requirements.title: The Source Connection ID field MUST be between 0 and 160 bits long
requirements.statement: The Source Connection ID field MUST be between 0 and 160 bits long.

requirements.title: Long headers MUST be used for packets that are sent prior to the establishment of 1-RTT keys
requirements.statement: Long headers MUST be used for packets that are sent prior to the establishment of 1-RTT keys.

requirements.title: Once 1-RTT keys are available, a sender MUST switch to sending packets using the short header...
requirements.statement: Once 1-RTT keys are available, a sender MUST switch to sending packets using the short header (Section 17.3).

requirements.title: Packets that use the long header MUST contain the following fields:
requirements.statement: Packets that use the long header MUST contain the following fields:

requirements.title: The most significant bit (0x80) of byte 0 (the first byte) MUST be set to 1 for long headers
requirements.statement: The most significant bit (0x80) of byte 0 (the first byte) MUST be set to 1 for long headers.

requirements.title: The next bit (0x40) of byte 0 MUST be set to 1, unless the packet is a Version Negotiation pa...
requirements.statement: The next bit (0x40) of byte 0 MUST be set to 1, unless the packet is a Version Negotiation packet.

requirements.title: Packets containing a zero value for this bit are not valid packets in this version and MUST b...
requirements.statement: Packets containing a zero value for this bit are not valid packets in this version and MUST be discarded.

requirements.title: The next two bits (those with a mask of 0x30) of byte 0 MUST contain a packet type
requirements.statement: The next two bits (those with a mask of 0x30) of byte 0 MUST contain a packet type.

requirements.title: This field MUST indicate the version of QUIC that is in use and determines how the rest of th...
requirements.statement: This field MUST indicate the version of QUIC that is in use and determines how the rest of the protocol fields are interpreted.

requirements.title: The byte following the version MUST contain the length in bytes of the Destination Connection...
requirements.statement: The byte following the version MUST contain the length in bytes of the Destination Connection ID field that follows it.

requirements.title: This length MUST be encoded as an 8-bit unsigned integer
requirements.statement: This length MUST be encoded as an 8-bit unsigned integer.

requirements.title: In QUIC version 1, this value MUST NOT exceed 20 bytes
requirements.statement: In QUIC version 1, this value MUST NOT exceed 20 bytes.

requirements.title: Endpoints that receive a version 1 long header with a value larger than 20 MUST drop the packet
requirements.statement: Endpoints that receive a version 1 long header with a value larger than 20 MUST drop the packet.

requirements.title: In order to properly form a Version Negotiation packet, servers SHOULD be able to read longer...
requirements.statement: In order to properly form a Version Negotiation packet, servers SHOULD be able to read longer connection IDs from other QUIC versions.

requirements.title: The Destination Connection ID field follows the Destination Connection ID Length field, which...
requirements.statement: The Destination Connection ID field follows the Destination Connection ID Length field, which MUST indicate the length of this field.

requirements.title: The byte following the Destination Connection ID MUST contain the length in bytes of the Sour...
requirements.statement: The byte following the Destination Connection ID MUST contain the length in bytes of the Source Connection ID field that follows it.

requirements.title: The Source Connection ID field follows the Source Connection ID Length field, which MUST indi...
requirements.statement: The Source Connection ID field follows the Source Connection ID Length field, which MUST indicate the length of this field.

requirements.title: While type-specific semantics for this version are described in the following sections, sever...
requirements.statement: While type-specific semantics for this version are described in the following sections, several long header packets in this version of QUIC MUST contain these additional fields:

requirements.title: Two bits (those with a mask of 0x0c) of byte 0 MUST be reserved across multiple packet types
requirements.statement: Two bits (those with a mask of 0x0c) of byte 0 MUST be reserved across multiple packet types.

requirements.title: The value included prior to protection MUST be set to 0
requirements.statement: The value included prior to protection MUST be set to 0.

requirements.title: An endpoint MUST treat receipt of a packet that has a non-zero value for these bits after rem...
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST treat receipt of a packet that has a non-zero value for these bits after removing both packet and header protection as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

requirements.title: Discarding such a packet after only removing header protection MAY expose the endpoint to att...
requirements.statement: Discarding such a packet after only removing header protection MAY expose the endpoint to attacks; see Section 9.5 of [QUIC-TLS].

requirements.title: In packet types that MUST contain a Packet Number field, the least significant two bits (thos...
requirements.statement: In packet types that MUST contain a Packet Number field, the least significant two bits (those with a mask of 0x03) of byte 0 contain the length of the Packet Number field, encoded as an unsigned two-bit integer that is one less than the length of the Packet Number field in bytes.

requirements.title: The Packet Number field MUST be field is 1 to 4 bytes long
requirements.statement: The Packet Number field MUST be field is 1 to 4 bytes long.

requirements.title: The length of the Packet Number field MUST be encoded in the Packet Number Length bits of byt...
requirements.statement: The length of the Packet Number field MUST be encoded in the Packet Number Length bits of byte 0; see above.

requirements.title: The Version Negotiation packet is a response to a client packet that contains a version that...
requirements.statement: The Version Negotiation packet is a response to a client packet that contains a version that MUST NOT be supported by the server.

requirements.title: It MUST only be sent by servers
requirements.statement: It MUST only be sent by servers.

requirements.title: The Header Form field MUST be 1 bits long with value 1
requirements.statement: The Header Form field MUST be 1 bits long with value 1.

requirements.title: The Unused field MUST be 7 bits long
requirements.statement: The Unused field MUST be 7 bits long.

requirements.title: The Version field MUST be 32 bits long with value 0
requirements.statement: The Version field MUST be 32 bits long with value 0.
