requirements.title: This section lists the defined QUIC transport error codes that MAY be used in a CONNECTION_CL...
requirements.statement: This section lists the defined QUIC transport error codes that MAY be used in a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame with a type of 0x1c.

requirements.title: An endpoint MUST use this with CONNECTION_CLOSE to signal that the connection is being closed...
requirements.statement: An endpoint MUST use this with CONNECTION_CLOSE to signal that the connection is being closed abruptly in the absence of any error.

requirements.title: The endpoint encountered an internal error and MUST NOT continue with the connection
requirements.statement: The endpoint encountered an internal error and MUST NOT continue with the connection.

requirements.title: An endpoint has received more data in CRYPTO frames than it MAY buffer
requirements.statement: An endpoint has received more data in CRYPTO frames than it MAY buffer.

requirements.title: An endpoint is unlikely to receive a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame carrying this code except when th...
requirements.statement: An endpoint is unlikely to receive a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame carrying this code except when the path MUST NOT support a large enough MTU.

requirements.title: A range of 256 values is reserved for carrying error codes specific to the cryptographic hand...
requirements.statement: A range of 256 values is reserved for carrying error codes specific to the cryptographic handshake that MUST be used.

requirements.title: Codes for errors occurring when TLS MUST be used for the cryptographic handshake are describe...
requirements.statement: Codes for errors occurring when TLS MUST be used for the cryptographic handshake are described in Section 4.8 of [QUIC-TLS].

requirements.title: Absent either of these conditions, error codes MUST be used to identify a general function of...
requirements.statement: Absent either of these conditions, error codes MUST be used to identify a general function of the stack, like flow control or transport parameter handling.

requirements.title: Application protocol error codes MUST be used for the RESET_STREAM frame (Section 19.4), the...
requirements.statement: Application protocol error codes MUST be used for the RESET_STREAM frame (Section 19.4), the STOP_SENDING frame (Section 19.5), and the CONNECTION_CLOSE frame with a type of 0x1d (Section 19.19).

requirements.title: Use address validation to confirm receipt capability
requirements.statement: Address validation (Section 8) MUST be used to verify that an entity that claims a given address is able to receive packets at that address.

requirements.title: Limit bytes sent to unvalidated addresses
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST NOT send data toward an unvalidated address in excess of three times the data received from that address.

requirements.title: Disallow address change during handshake
requirements.statement: Addresses MUST NOT change during the handshake.
notes: The source sentence also notes that endpoints can discard packets received on a different network path.

requirements.title: Discard packets that cannot be authenticated
requirements.statement: Packets that cannot be authenticated MUST be discarded.

requirements.title: Servers SHOULD provide mitigations for this attack by limiting the usage and lifetime of addr...
requirements.statement: Servers SHOULD provide mitigations for this attack by limiting the usage and lifetime of address validation tokens; see Section 8.1.3.

requirements.title: An endpoint MAY skip packet numbers when sending packets to detect this behavior
requirements.statement: An endpoint MAY skip packet numbers when sending packets to detect this behavior.

requirements.title: For request forgery to be effective, an attacker MUST be able to influence what packets the p...
requirements.statement: For request forgery to be effective, an attacker MUST be able to influence what packets the peer sends and where these packets are sent.

requirements.title: QUIC servers SHOULD NOT be deployed in networks that do not deploy ingress filtering [BCP38]...
requirements.statement: QUIC servers SHOULD NOT be deployed in networks that do not deploy ingress filtering [BCP38] and also have inadequately secured UDP endpoints.

requirements.title: Any future extension that allows server migration MUST also define countermeasures for forger...
requirements.statement: Any future extension that allows server migration MUST also define countermeasures for forgery attacks.

requirements.title: A client MUST NOT send non-probing frames to a preferred address prior to validating that add...
requirements.statement: A client MUST NOT send non-probing frames to a preferred address prior to validating that address; see Section 8.

requirements.title: Endpoints MAY prevent connection attempts or migration to a loopback address
requirements.statement: Endpoints MAY prevent connection attempts or migration to a loopback address.

requirements.title: Endpoints SHOULD NOT allow connections or migration to a loopback address if the same service...
requirements.statement: Endpoints SHOULD NOT allow connections or migration to a loopback address if the same service was previously available at a different interface or if the address was provided by a service at a non-loopback address.

requirements.title: Endpoints SHOULD NOT refuse to use an address unless they have specific knowledge about the n...
requirements.statement: Endpoints SHOULD NOT refuse to use an address unless they have specific knowledge about the network indicating that sending datagrams to unvalidated addresses in a given range is not safe.

requirements.title: Endpoints MAY choose to reduce the risk of request forgery by not including values from NEW_T...
requirements.statement: Endpoints MAY choose to reduce the risk of request forgery by not including values from NEW_TOKEN frames in Initial packets or by only sending probing frames in packets prior to completing address validation.

requirements.title: Endpoints MAY choose to avoid sending datagrams to these ports or not send datagrams that mat...
requirements.statement: Endpoints MAY choose to avoid sending datagrams to these ports or not send datagrams that match these patterns prior to validating the destination address.

requirements.title: Endpoints MAY retire connection IDs containing patterns known to be problematic without using...
requirements.statement: Endpoints MAY retire connection IDs containing patterns known to be problematic without using them.

requirements.title: QUIC deployments SHOULD provide mitigations for the Slowloris attacks, such as increasing the...
requirements.statement: QUIC deployments SHOULD provide mitigations for the Slowloris attacks, such as increasing the maximum number of clients the server will allow, limiting the number of connections a single IP address is allowed to make, imposing restrictions on the minimum transfer speed a connection is allowed to have, and restricting the length of time an endpoint is allowed to stay connected.

requirements.title: QUIC deployments SHOULD provide mitigations for stream fragmentation attacks
requirements.statement: QUIC deployments SHOULD provide mitigations for stream fragmentation attacks.

requirements.title: While there are legitimate uses for all messages, implementations SHOULD track cost of proces...
requirements.statement: While there are legitimate uses for all messages, implementations SHOULD track cost of processing relative to progress and treat excessive quantities of any non-productive packets as indicative of an attack.

requirements.title: Endpoints MAY respond to this condition with a connection error or by dropping packets
requirements.statement: Endpoints MAY respond to this condition with a connection error or by dropping packets.

requirements.title: If duplicate packets are discarded by a receiver, an attacker will MUST race the duplicate pa...
requirements.statement: If duplicate packets are discarded by a receiver, an attacker will MUST race the duplicate packet against the original to be successful in this attack.

requirements.title: To defend against this style of denial of service, endpoints that share a static key for stat...
requirements.statement: To defend against this style of denial of service, endpoints that share a static key for stateless resets (see Section 10.3.2) MUST be arranged so that packets with a given connection ID always arrive at an instance that has connection state, unless that connection is no longer active.

requirements.title: More generally, servers MUST NOT generate a stateless reset if a connection with the correspo...
requirements.statement: More generally, servers MUST NOT generate a stateless reset if a connection with the corresponding connection ID could be active on any endpoint using the same static key.

requirements.title: Keep Version Negotiation packets free of downgrade prevention mechanisms
requirements.statement: Version Negotiation packets MUST NOT contain any mechanism to prevent version downgrade attacks.

requirements.title: Future versions of QUIC that use Version Negotiation packets MUST define a mechanism that is...
requirements.statement: Future versions of QUIC that use Version Negotiation packets MUST define a mechanism that is robust against version downgrade attacks.

requirements.title: Limit provisional registrations to codepoint value and contact information
requirements.statement: Provisional registrations MUST include only the codepoint value and contact information.

requirements.title: Require Expert Review for provisional registrations
requirements.statement: Provisional registrations MUST undergo Expert Review, as defined in Section 4.5 of [RFC8126].

requirements.title: Include a Date field in provisional registrations
requirements.statement: Provisional registrations MUST include a Date field that indicates when the registration was last updated.

requirements.title: Allow provisional date updates without expert review
requirements.statement: A request to update the date on any provisional registration MAY be made without review from the designated expert(s).

requirements.title: Include provisional registration support fields
requirements.statement: All QUIC registries MUST include the following fields to support provisional registration.

requirements.title: Define the Value field
requirements.statement: The Value field MUST be the assigned codepoint.

requirements.title: Define the Status field
requirements.statement: The Status field MUST be "permanent" or "provisional".

requirements.title: Define the Specification field
requirements.statement: The Specification field MUST be a reference to a publicly available specification for the value.

requirements.title: Define the Date field
requirements.statement: The Date field MUST be the date of the last update to the registration.

requirements.title: Define the Change Controller field
requirements.statement: The Change Controller field MUST be the entity responsible for the definition of the registration.

requirements.title: Define the Contact field
requirements.statement: The Contact field MUST be contact details for the registrant.

requirements.title: Define the Notes field
requirements.statement: The Notes field MUST be supplementary notes about the registration.

requirements.title: Allow provisional omission of selected registry fields
requirements.statement: Provisional registrations MAY omit the Specification and Notes fields, plus any additional fields that might be required for a permanent registration.

requirements.title: Do not require Date when requesting registration
requirements.statement: The Date field MUST NOT be required as part of requesting a registration.

requirements.title: New requests for codepoints from QUIC registries SHOULD use a randomly selected codepoint tha...
requirements.statement: New requests for codepoints from QUIC registries SHOULD use a randomly selected codepoint that excludes both existing allocations and the first unallocated codepoint in the selected space.

requirements.title: Requests for multiple codepoints MAY use a contiguous range
requirements.statement: Requests for multiple codepoints MAY use a contiguous range.

requirements.title: Reserve first unassigned codepoint for Standards Action
requirements.statement: The use of the first unassigned codepoint MUST be reserved for allocation using the Standards Action policy.

requirements.title: Allow early codepoint assignment for selected values
requirements.statement: The early codepoint assignment process MAY be used for these values.

requirements.title: For codepoints that are encoded in variable-length integers (Section 16), such as frame types...
requirements.statement: For codepoints that are encoded in variable-length integers (Section 16), such as frame types, codepoints that encode to four or eight bytes (that is, values 214 and above) SHOULD be used unless the usage is especially sensitive to having a longer encoding.

requirements.title: Applications to register codepoints in QUIC registries MAY include a requested codepoint as p...
requirements.statement: Applications to register codepoints in QUIC registries MAY include a requested codepoint as part of the registration.

requirements.title: IANA MUST allocate the selected codepoint if the codepoint is unassigned and the requirements...
requirements.statement: IANA MUST allocate the selected codepoint if the codepoint is unassigned and the requirements of the registration policy are met.

requirements.title: A request to remove a codepoint MUST be reviewed by the designated experts
requirements.statement: A request to remove a codepoint MUST be reviewed by the designated experts.

requirements.title: The experts MUST attempt to determine whether the codepoint is still in use
requirements.statement: The experts MUST attempt to determine whether the codepoint is still in use.

requirements.title: If any use of the codepoints is identified by this search or a request to update the registra...
requirements.statement: If any use of the codepoints is identified by this search or a request to update the registration is made, the codepoint MUST NOT be reclaimed.

requirements.title: If no use of the codepoint was identified and no request was made to update the registration,...
requirements.statement: If no use of the codepoint was identified and no request was made to update the registration, the codepoint MAY be removed from the registry.

requirements.title: Use Specification Required for permanent registrations
requirements.statement: Permanent registrations in QUIC registries MUST use the Specification Required policy (Section 4.6 of [RFC8126]), unless otherwise specified.

requirements.title: Verify the specification for permanent registrations
requirements.statement: The designated expert or experts MUST verify that a specification exists and is readily accessible.

requirements.title: The creation of a registry MAY specify additional constraints on permanent registrations
requirements.statement: The creation of a registry MAY specify additional constraints on permanent registrations.

requirements.title: The creation of a registry MAY identify a range of codepoints where registrations are governe...
requirements.statement: The creation of a registry MAY identify a range of codepoints where registrations are governed by a different registration policy.

requirements.title: Do not block provisional registrations with stricter permanent requirements
requirements.statement: Any stricter requirements for permanent registrations MUST NOT prevent provisional registrations for affected codepoints.

requirements.title: Make Standards Track registrations permanent
requirements.statement: All registrations made by Standards Track publications MUST be permanent.

requirements.title: Assign document registrations permanent status
requirements.statement: All registrations in this document MUST be assigned a permanent status.

requirements.title: Record the change controller and contact for document registrations
requirements.statement: All registrations in this document MUST list a change controller of the IETF and a contact of the QUIC Working Group (quic@ietf.org).

requirements.title: Assign codepoint 0x00000001
requirements.statement: The codepoint of 0x00000001 for the protocol MUST be assigned with permanent status to the protocol defined in this document.

requirements.title: Reserve codepoint 0x00000000
requirements.statement: The codepoint of 0x00000000 MUST be permanently reserved.
notes: The source sentence also notes that the registry note indicates this version is reserved for version negotiation.

requirements.title: Exclude reserved version-pattern codepoints from listings
requirements.statement: All codepoints that follow the pattern 0x?a?a?a?a MUST NOT appear in the listing of assigned values.

requirements.title: Protect reserved version-pattern codepoints
requirements.statement: All codepoints that follow the pattern 0x?a?a?a?a MUST NOT be assigned by IANA.

requirements.title: Use Specification Required for transport parameters
requirements.statement: Permanent registrations in this registry MUST be assigned using the Specification Required policy.

requirements.title: Use stricter policy for low transport-parameter codepoints
requirements.statement: Values between 0x00 and 0x3f inclusive MUST be assigned using Standards Action or IESG Approval.

requirements.title: Include Parameter Name for transport parameters
requirements.statement: Permanent registrations in this registry MUST include the Parameter Name field.

requirements.title: Define Parameter Name
requirements.statement: The Parameter Name field MUST be a short mnemonic for the parameter.

requirements.title: Use Specification Required for frame types
requirements.statement: Permanent registrations in this registry MUST be assigned using the Specification Required policy.

requirements.title: Use stricter policy for low frame-type codepoints
requirements.statement: Values between 0x00 and 0x3f inclusive MUST be assigned using Standards Action or IESG Approval.

requirements.title: Include Frame Type Name for frame types
requirements.statement: Permanent registrations in this registry MUST include the Frame Type Name field.

requirements.title: Define Frame Type Name
requirements.statement: The Frame Type Name field MUST be a short mnemonic for the frame type.

requirements.title: In addition to the advice in Section 22.1, specifications for new permanent registrations SHO...
requirements.statement: In addition to the advice in Section 22.1, specifications for new permanent registrations SHOULD describe the means by which an endpoint might determine that it can send the identified type of frame.

requirements.title: Describe frame field semantics in specifications
requirements.statement: Specifications for permanent registrations MUST describe the format and assigned semantics of any fields in the frame.

requirements.title: Use Specification Required for transport error codes
requirements.statement: Permanent registrations in this registry MUST be assigned using the Specification Required policy.

requirements.title: Use stricter policy for low transport error codepoints
requirements.statement: Values between 0x00 and 0x3f inclusive MUST be assigned using Standards Action or IESG Approval.

requirements.title: Include Code and Description for transport error codes
requirements.statement: Permanent registrations in this registry MUST include the following fields.

requirements.title: Define Code
requirements.statement: The Code field MUST be a short mnemonic for the parameter.

requirements.title: Define Description
requirements.statement: The Description field MUST be a brief description of the error code semantics.
notes: The source sentence allows this to be a summary if a specification reference is provided.

title: QUIC Transport (RFC 9000)
